File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:130:+4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:131:+5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:219:+`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1040:+4. **Refused for privacy-sensitive modes** — `PublishPrivacy::PrivateToRecipients` rejects an override that adds non-inbox relays. The override may *narrow* a private fan-out to a subset of declared inboxes; it may not *widen* to public relays.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1230:+Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1268:+M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1272:+The actor's planner inbox is a FIFO queue. Order matters only at the granularity of a tick: within a tick, all queued triggers are folded into the compile inputs and one compile runs. Across ticks, recompiles happen in order received.

The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1713:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:109:Outbox support belongs in the actor-owned routing and planning layer. Relay sockets should not decide whether a relay is an inbox, outbox, indexer, fallback, or hint. They should connect, subscribe, publish, report status, and nothing more.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1716:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:159:NMP should therefore make outbox invisible in ordinary app code but visible in diagnostics and tests. It should use explicit internal routing artifacts, but expose intent-oriented product APIs. It should route efficiently by author and recipient, but still render cached state immediately. It should support fallback for public reads, but fail closed for private delivery. It should make NIP-77 a coverage and backfill policy, not a blocking fetch API. It should compile logical interests into cache, sync, live, and fallback work without exposing that machinery to platform code.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1717:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:161:That is the durable lesson: outbox support is not a feature bolted onto subscriptions. It is a framework-level correctness policy that touches storage, planning, publishing, diagnostics, privacy, and the developer API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1729:docs/aim.md:127:The framework implements the outbox model **by default and automatically**. Subscriptions with `authors` filters automatically route reads to those authors' write relays. Publishes for an event automatically go to the author's write relays plus inbox relays of any `p`-tagged recipients (for DMs and notifications). The developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does. They can override, but the override is the exception.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1775:docs/plan.md:299:- `nmp-nip17` protocol module: Conversation view module + ConversationList view module; SendDm action module; NIP-44 encryption / NIP-59 gift-wrapping; outbox routing for DMs (recipient inbox relays only — never public).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1920:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:13:- Add their app nouns (`Highlight`, `Project`, `Episode`, `WeightLog`, `DailyPlan`) to `nmp-core`. The framework becomes a junk drawer of every consumer's domain concepts. Untenable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1957:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:130:| 11. Messaging | NIP-17/NIP-44/NIP-59, conversation store, background decrypt crate | Missing DM inbox relays fail closed; module owns no separate relay pool or persistence engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1984:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:81:| Subscription with `p` tag filter or notifications | Union of each tagged pubkey's inbox relays. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1987:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:84:| Publish with `p` tags (DMs, mentions, reactions) | Author's write relays **plus** each tagged pubkey's inbox relays. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1988:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:85:| DM (NIP-17 gift-wrapped) | **Only** resolved recipient inbox relays. Never the author's write relays. Never the active session's "default" relays. Missing recipient inbox relays fail closed. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:91:- "DM accidentally public" → ruled out by the safe API. The DM publish path consults only resolved inbox relays; there is no fallback-to-all-relays path for gift wraps.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2011:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:318:The module is intentionally stricter than common fallback patterns: if recipient inbox relays are unknown, a DM send action fails closed with diagnostic state instead of publishing gift wraps to default/public relays. Read state is local conversation state unless and until an explicit protocol-level receipt policy is added.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2030:docs/design/view-catalog/template-and-enumeration.md:9:> **Status:** Rev 2, reframed per ADR-0009. These view kinds are not in `nmp-core`; apps consume them by adding the owning module crate to `nmp.toml` and regenerating the per-app FFI crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2045:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:15:Parsed kind:10002 for one pubkey. Payload: `MailboxesView { pubkey, inbox_relays: Vec<String>, outbox_relays: Vec<String>, raw_event_id }`. Phase 1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2050:docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:10:ADR-0009 establishes that NMP is a kernel with extension modules. Apps assemble themselves from a kernel (`nmp-core`), a set of protocol modules (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, ...), and an app-specific core crate (`twitter-core`, `highlighter-core`, ...). Each layer can define typed `AppAction` variants, `AppUpdate` variants, `ViewSpec` variants, and so on.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2063:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2064:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:33:Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2068:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:62:Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2090:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:5:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 (the slice is now built on top of the kernel substrate; the kind:0 path is a Profile `ViewModule` in `nmp-nip01`, not a built-in feature of `nmp-core`)

This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2479:   133	- **Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` check** is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #4 (`docs/plan.md` line 306 — "DM to public: no API path can send a DM to a non-inbox relay"). Indexer-sourced inbox means we have no NIP-65-declared inbox; for private events that is fail-closed. The recipient gets nothing rather than getting a public broadcast.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2489:   143	4. **Refused for privacy-sensitive modes** — `PublishPrivacy::PrivateToRecipients` rejects an override that adds non-inbox relays. The override may *narrow* a private fan-out to a subset of declared inboxes; it may not *widen* to public relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2530:   184	        // ... validate that inner action's privacy mode permits override ...
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2654:    80	`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3068:   103	Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3106:   141	M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3110:   145	The actor's planner inbox is a FIFO queue. Order matters only at the granularity of a tick: within a tick, all queued triggers are folded into the compile inputs and one compile runs. Across ticks, recompiles happen in order received.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3445:    40	4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3446:    41	5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3947:     9	> **Status:** Rev 2, reframed per ADR-0009. These view kinds are not in `nmp-core`; apps consume them by adding the owning module crate to `nmp.toml` and regenerating the per-app FFI crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4037:   109	Outbox support belongs in the actor-owned routing and planning layer. Relay sockets should not decide whether a relay is an inbox, outbox, indexer, fallback, or hint. They should connect, subscribe, publish, report status, and nothing more.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4385:- [outbox.md:103](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:103): override returns before privacy validation, conflicting with [outbox.md:138](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138). Fix by deriving the allowed base set first, then validate override as a subset for private routes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4399:- [outbox.md:119](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:119): private publish uses a union of recipient inbox relays plus `required_success_count = recipients.len() as u8`; this neither proves one success per recipient nor avoids overflow. Fix with per-recipient delivery requirements and `usize`.

docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:279:The starter app on first launch presents login. Logging in with a private key or pairing a bunker yields a working following-timeline with live updates, compose, profile, and DMs.
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:4:**Status:** accepted (discipline preserved; positioning modified by ADR-0009)
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:5:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 (the slice is now built on top of the kernel substrate; the kind:0 path is a Profile `ViewModule` in `nmp-nip01`, not a built-in feature of `nmp-core`)
docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:530:    ✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:780:d660735:docs/aim.md:127:The framework implements the outbox model **by default and automatically**. Subscriptions with `authors` filters automatically route reads to those authors' write relays. Publishes for an event automatically go to the author's write relays plus inbox relays of any `p`-tagged recipients (for DMs and notifications). The developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does. They can override, but the override is the exception.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:784:d660735:docs/aim.md:139:Account persistence is automatic via the OS keyring crate (Keychain / Credential Manager / Secret Service). Signers cover the spectrum: private key (encrypted at rest), NIP-49 password-encrypted, NIP-07 browser extension (with a proxy for native apps), NIP-46 bunker (Nostr Connect), and platform-native external signers (e.g. the Android Amber app via NIP-55, a capability bridge in RMP terms).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:866:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:391:- **Capabilities are module-private.** A capability registered by Module A is callable only by code that has a typed handle to it. The kernel does not route across.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:878:d660735:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:109:Outbox support belongs in the actor-owned routing and planning layer. Relay sockets should not decide whether a relay is an inbox, outbox, indexer, fallback, or hint. They should connect, subscribe, publish, report status, and nothing more.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:880:d660735:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:159:NMP should therefore make outbox invisible in ordinary app code but visible in diagnostics and tests. It should use explicit internal routing artifacts, but expose intent-oriented product APIs. It should route efficiently by author and recipient, but still render cached state immediately. It should support fallback for public reads, but fail closed for private delivery. It should make NIP-77 a coverage and backfill policy, not a blocking fetch API. It should compile logical interests into cache, sync, live, and fallback work without exposing that machinery to platform code.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:881:d660735:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:161:That is the durable lesson: outbox support is not a feature bolted onto subscriptions. It is a framework-level correctness policy that touches storage, planning, publishing, diagnostics, privacy, and the developer API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:891:d660735:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:15:Parsed kind:10002 for one pubkey. Payload: `MailboxesView { pubkey, inbox_relays: Vec<String>, outbox_relays: Vec<String>, raw_event_id }`. Phase 1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:948:d660735:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:967:d660735:docs/plan.md:299:- `nmp-nip17` protocol module: Conversation view module + ConversationList view module; SendDm action module; NIP-44 encryption / NIP-59 gift-wrapping; outbox routing for DMs (recipient inbox relays only — never public).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1120:    62	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1158:    13	- Add their app nouns (`Highlight`, `Project`, `Episode`, `WeightLog`, `DailyPlan`) to `nmp-core`. The framework becomes a junk drawer of every consumer's domain concepts. Untenable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1191:    46	| Layer | Owns | May contain app nouns? |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1622:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:272-282`: D0 is incorrectly marked compliant. The doc itself cites `Profile` and `TimelineItem` inside `nmp-core`, and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:57-114` contains `Profile`, `TimelineItem`, `ProfileCard`, `AuthorViewPayload`, and `ThreadViewPayload`. Per ADR-0009, `nmp-core` must not contain Profile/Timeline/Thread view-kind business logic. Fix the audit to mark this as blocking D0 debt or move those nouns out before signoff.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1633:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:272-282`: D0 is incorrectly marked compliant. The doc itself cites `Profile` and `TimelineItem` inside `nmp-core`, and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:57-114` contains `Profile`, `TimelineItem`, `ProfileCard`, `AuthorViewPayload`, and `ThreadViewPayload`. Per ADR-0009, `nmp-core` must not contain Profile/Timeline/Thread view-kind business logic. Fix the audit to mark this as blocking D0 debt or move those nouns out before signoff.
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:6:**Depends on:** ADR-0009 (kernel boundary)
docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:10:ADR-0009 establishes that NMP is a kernel with extension modules. Apps assemble themselves from a kernel (`nmp-core`), a set of protocol modules (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, ...), and an app-specific core crate (`twitter-core`, `highlighter-core`, ...). Each layer can define typed `AppAction` variants, `AppUpdate` variants, `ViewSpec` variants, and so on.
docs/perf/ios-demo/2026-05-17-profile-thread-subscriptions.md:113:- Later: write/inbox/media roles.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:85:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:103:+7. `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:251:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:254:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:404:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (the resolution algorithm — read & write rows); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` (the `PublishPlanner` trait); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3 (the read-side compiler pipeline); `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.5 (privacy-sensitive routes fail closed).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:406:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:430:+## C7. Write fan-out: outbox + recipient-inbox; private events fail closed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:432:+**Statement.** Every publish action's signed event is routed by the `PublishPlanner` (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1) according to a `PublishPrivacy` mode the action declares. **Public** events go to author write relays. **PublicWithNotifications** events go to author writes ∪ recipient inboxes (`#p` tagged pubkeys). **PrivateToRecipients** events (gift-wrapped per NIP-59) go to **only** resolved recipient inbox relays — never the author's writes, never the active session's defaults, never the indexer set. If any recipient has no declared inbox, the publish fails closed with `PublishPlanError::PrivateRecipientUnroutable`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:437:+- Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` check on recipient inbox lookups is the structural fail-closed for private events.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:440:+**App writes:** nothing — for the publish path. The app dispatches a publish action (`SendNote`, `React`, `SendDm`, etc.); the action's privacy mode is determined by the action type, not by an app-supplied parameter. There is no `relays` field on `SendNote`. The override exists for tests, migrations, and operator power-user flows; it is structurally outside the safe app path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:442:+**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:444:+**Test:** `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`. The test has three sub-paths:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:447:+2. **PublicWithNotifications:** dispatch a note tagging Bob (Bob has one inbox relay seeded); assert the plan is Alice's writes ∪ Bob's inbox, with the correct `PublishRouteReason::AuthorWriteRelay` / `RecipientInbox` tagging per assignment.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:449:+4. **Override rejection:** dispatch a `PublishWithOverride` carrying a `PrivateToRecipients` inner action and an override relay set that includes a non-inbox URL; assert it rejects with `PublishPlanError::OverrideRejected { reason: "private widen" }` (rule 4 of `outbox.md` §7.4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:891:+c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:981:+        "c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed",
docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1060:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance — does the framework-magic contract violate D0 by adding app nouns to the kernel, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget per file, (4) internal consistency of the contract (are the 14 tests coherent with the contract). Be terse. Name file:line for any real concern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:105:+5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:139:+**Scope.** Per ADR-0006 + ADR-0008 + ADR-0009: kind:0 Profile path end-to-end against a real relay, on iOS, through real FFI. Seed-driven discovery (union of follow lists from pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55). Refcounted claim/release pattern per ADR-0005 (profile interest commit `23ae829`). Diagnostics surface per ADR-0007.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:233:-Per ADR-0006 (slice discipline), ADR-0008 (Twitter clone iOS target), ADR-0009 (kernel + extension modules), and ADR-0010 (generated app enum), Phase 1 grows the kernel substrate first, then layers Nostr protocol modules and the Twitter clone on top. Every sub-phase has running code at its exit gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:285:+**Demo product:** iOS app connects to an NIP-42-required relay (such as a private nostr.wine subscription) and successfully authenticates + receives content.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:340:+- `KeychainCapability` real implementation: encrypted nsec storage via iOS Keychain, app-private access group.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:398:-- NIP-49 encrypted private key support.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:404:+- View specs that depend on the active account (Timeline of "your follows", DMs inbox, zap history) get account-scoped composite keys.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:445:+- `nmp-nip17` protocol module: Conversation view module + ConversationList view module; SendDm action module; NIP-44 encryption / NIP-59 gift-wrapping; outbox routing for DMs (recipient inbox relays only — never public).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:448:+- Action atomicity for `SendDm`: gift-wrap → publish to all recipient inboxes → insert locally — atomic.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:462:+- Bug-extinction #4 (DM to public): no API path can send a DM to a non-inbox relay; planner refuses non-inbox relays for `p`-tagged-only events.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:485:-- Action atomicity for `SendDm`: gift-wrap, publish to each recipient's inbox relays, insert locally — atomic.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:500:-- Bug-extinction test #4 (DM to public): cannot send a DM to a non-inbox relay through any public API path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:896:-- **ADR-0008:** Phase 1a demo target is a simple Twitter-clone iOS app pulling from primal, with seed-driven timeline discovery (union of follow lists of hardcoded dev accounts) as the unauthenticated default. Sub-phases, each a walking skeleton. Desktop iced reference target preserved alongside iOS for UniFFI-vs-architecture debugging. Supersedes ADR-0006 in choice of demo target only; discipline preserved. Modified by ADR-0009.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:897:-- **ADR-0009:** App extension kernel boundary. NMP is reframed as a Nostr-native app kernel with five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`). The kernel owns substrate; protocol modules and app crates own nouns. Closed enums in the API are replaced by per-app generated enums (see ADR-0010). Phase 1a restructured to build the kernel substrate (with a non-Nostr fixture module) before the first Nostr protocol module. Adopted 2026-05-17 from `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:957:+- **ADR-0006**: Vertical-slice-first delivery (modified by ADR-0009; the slice now layers on the kernel substrate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:959:+- **ADR-0008**: Twitter-clone iOS as the Phase 1a demo target (modified by ADR-0009 — repositioned as first canonical extension-module set).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:960:+- **ADR-0009**: App-extension kernel boundary. Five trait families, four layers, no app nouns in nmp-core.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:90:+1. **Applesauce explorer** — full deep-dive on `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` (git-tracked, May 2026). docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:99:+- DMs deferred ≠ no privacy at v1. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays (M2) stays. When DMs land post-v1, they slot in cleanly because the routing contract is already there.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:306:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:125:Public timeline discovery can tolerate bounded fallback. Private or recipient-sensitive publishing cannot. DMs, gift wraps, and other private flows must not silently fall back to broad public relays because metadata was missing.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:319:docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:397:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:413:docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:103:Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:418:docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:141:M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:434:docs/plan/m9-messaging.md:11:- `nmp-nip17` protocol module: Conversation view module + ConversationList view module; SendDm action module; NIP-44 encryption / NIP-59 gift-wrapping; outbox routing for DMs (recipient inbox relays only — never public).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:436:docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md:12:- View specs that depend on the active account (Timeline of "your follows", DMs inbox, zap history) get account-scoped composite keys.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:454:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:80:`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4054:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4056:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4085:    62	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.

docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:7:**Scope.** Per ADR-0006 + ADR-0008 + ADR-0009: kind:0 Profile path end-to-end against a real relay, on iOS, through real FFI. Seed-driven discovery (union of follow lists from pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55). Refcounted claim/release pattern per ADR-0005 (profile interest commit `23ae829`). Diagnostics surface per ADR-0007.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:162:pub fn resolve_author_inbox(
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:186:- **No outbox routing policy.** The decision "publish goes to author write relays + recipient inbox relays" is the publish planner ([outbox.md](outbox.md) §7), not this crate. This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:199:- `nmp-core::kernel::planner` — for `MailboxCache`, `resolve_author_outbox/inbox`, `parse_relay_list`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:201:- Future `nmp-nip17` (M9) — DM publish path imports `resolve_author_inbox` for recipient lookups.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:91:Compilation must be semantics-preserving. Some filters can be safely merged, split, or grouped. Others cannot. Limits, time bounds, close-on-EOSE behavior, relay exclusivity, private delivery, and author-specific routing can all change the meaning of a request if combined carelessly. The compiler should prefer doing less work over issuing an over-broad request that silently changes semantics.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:109:Outbox support belongs in the actor-owned routing and planning layer. Relay sockets should not decide whether a relay is an inbox, outbox, indexer, fallback, or hint. They should connect, subscribe, publish, report status, and nothing more.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:125:Public timeline discovery can tolerate bounded fallback. Private or recipient-sensitive publishing cannot. DMs, gift wraps, and other private flows must not silently fall back to broad public relays because metadata was missing.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:143:NMP should have tests that assert routing policy, not just end-to-end happy paths. The important failures are subtle: wrong relay category, over-broad fan-out, stale relay-list replacement, fallback that hides missing coverage, and private events delivered to public relays.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:159:NMP should therefore make outbox invisible in ordinary app code but visible in diagnostics and tests. It should use explicit internal routing artifacts, but expose intent-oriented product APIs. It should route efficiently by author and recipient, but still render cached state immediately. It should support fallback for public reads, but fail closed for private delivery. It should make NIP-77 a coverage and backfill policy, not a blocking fetch API. It should compile logical interests into cache, sync, live, and fallback work without exposing that machinery to platform code.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:161:That is the durable lesson: outbox support is not a feature bolted onto subscriptions. It is a framework-level correctness policy that touches storage, planning, publishing, diagnostics, privacy, and the developer API.
docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:4:**Status:** accepted (positioning modified by ADR-0009)
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:7:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 — the Twitter clone is now built as **the first canonical extension module set** (`twitter-core` app crate on top of `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / `nmp-nip10` / `nmp-nip25` protocol modules), not as a built-in feature of `nmp-core`. Sub-phase plan adjusted: 1a.0 (foundations) is followed by 1a.1 (**kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture module**) BEFORE 1a.2 (first Nostr protocol module + desktop avatar slice). Twitter clone features layer on from 1a.4 onward.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:24:- Log in with a private key (nsec or generated) to enable writes.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:104:- `KeychainCapability` real implementation: encrypted nsec storage via iOS Keychain (app-private access group for now; shared group for NSE in Phase 5).
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:144:| 1a.4 | **Signer trait shape + ActionCx fields** (currently sketched). **Login UX flow** (single screen vs onboarding wizard). **KeychainCapability iOS access groups** (app-private for now, named group placeholder for NSE). Pre-1a.4 deliverable. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:103:Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:141:M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:145:The actor's planner inbox is a FIFO queue. Order matters only at the granularity of a tick: within a tick, all queued triggers are folded into the compile inputs and one compile runs. Across ticks, recompiles happen in order received.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:80:+| `DomainModule` rows (drafts, settings, action ledger, projection caches) | Entirely out of scope; the crate stores Nostr `Event` only | ADR-0009 + `kernel-substrate.md` §2 — kernel hosts non-Nostr typed rows |

The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2309:    62	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2347:   130	- NIP-49 (password-encrypted private key)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2629:    81	| Subscription with `p` tag filter or notifications | Union of each tagged pubkey's inbox relays. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2632:    84	| Publish with `p` tags (DMs, mentions, reactions) | Author's write relays **plus** each tagged pubkey's inbox relays. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:2633:    85	| DM (NIP-17 gift-wrapped) | **Only** resolved recipient inbox relays. Never the author's write relays. Never the active session's "default" relays. Missing recipient inbox relays fail closed. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:3110:   232	10. **Provenance preserved.** Every event in the store remembers which relays delivered it; private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:100:+  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:107:+- **App-extension kernel boundary** (ADR-0009). NMP is a kernel + five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`). Per-app concrete enums generated at the FFI boundary via `nmp gen modules` (ADR-0010). Apps assemble themselves from `nmp-core` + protocol modules + their own `<app>-core` crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:109:+- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:205:+- [Outbox routing — read fan-out, write fan-out, private events fail closed](framework-magic/outbox.md) (C6, C7)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:243:+7. `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:544:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (the resolution algorithm — read & write rows); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` (the `PublishPlanner` trait); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3 (the read-side compiler pipeline); `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.5 (privacy-sensitive routes fail closed).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:570:+## C7. Write fan-out: outbox + recipient-inbox; private events fail closed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:572:+**Statement.** Every publish action's signed event is routed by the `PublishPlanner` (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1) according to a `PublishPrivacy` mode the action declares. **Public** events go to author write relays. **PublicWithNotifications** events go to author writes ∪ recipient inboxes (`#p` tagged pubkeys). **PrivateToRecipients** events (gift-wrapped per NIP-59) go to **only** resolved recipient inbox relays — never the author's writes, never the active session's defaults, never the indexer set. If any recipient has no declared inbox, the publish fails closed with `PublishPlanError::PrivateRecipientUnroutable`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:577:+- Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` check on recipient inbox lookups is the structural fail-closed for private events.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:580:+**App writes:** nothing — for the publish path. The app dispatches a publish action (`SendNote`, `React`, `SendDm`, etc.); the action's privacy mode is determined by the action type, not by an app-supplied parameter. There is no `relays` field on `SendNote`. The override exists for tests, migrations, and operator power-user flows; it is structurally outside the safe app path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:582:+**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:584:+**Test:** `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`. The test has three sub-paths:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:587:+2. **PublicWithNotifications:** dispatch a note tagging Bob (Bob has one inbox relay seeded); assert the plan is Alice's writes ∪ Bob's inbox, with the correct `PublishRouteReason::AuthorWriteRelay` / `RecipientInbox` tagging per assignment.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:589:+4. **Override rejection:** dispatch a `PublishWithOverride` carrying a `PrivateToRecipients` inner action and an override relay set that includes a non-inbox URL; assert it rejects with `PublishPlanError::OverrideRejected { reason: "private widen" }` (rule 4 of `outbox.md` §7.4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1031:+c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1121:+        "c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed",

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1270:+> Source: `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` @ `da5ec22b` (May 2026).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1433:+> Source: `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` @ `da5ec22b` (unshallowed: 1043 commits).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1594:+> Source: `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` @ `da5ec22b`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1614:+**NMP spec (`aim.md:123-125`):** "Publishes for an event automatically go to the author's write relays plus inbox relays of any p-tagged recipients. The developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does. They can override, but the override is the exception."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1616:+**Gap:** NMP must implement the default-on path. This means the planner inspects every publish, resolves author outboxes + recipient inboxes from the `OutboxState`, and routes automatically. Explicit-override is the opt-out.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1719:+> Source: `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` @ `da5ec22b`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1731:+  .pipe(map(event => event && { inboxes: getInboxes(event), outboxes: getOutboxes(event) }));
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1734:+Reads a kind:10002 via the generic `replaceable()` query-builder and projects into `{inboxes, outboxes}`. Re-emits on any kind:10002 update.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1858:+Symmetrically, `MailboxesModel` returns `inboxes` and `includeMailboxes(store, 'inbox')` does the same composition for the inbox side. NMP's deferred DM milestone needs to wire publishes to a recipient via `addressable -> recipient kind:10002 -> inboxes`. Applesauce expects the caller to do this manually per-action; NMP should bake it into the publish path itself when DMs un-defer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1872:+4. The "publish to outbox + recipient inboxes by default" guarantee is **stronger than Applesauce's**. Don't copy Applesauce's caller-responsibility model — bake it into the planner.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1881:+> Source: `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` @ `da5ec22b`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1902:+| `PrivateKeySigner` | 38 | `signers/private-key-signer.ts` | Plain in-memory key. NIP-04 + NIP-44 via nostr-tools. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1915:+## 3. `PrivateKeySigner` — the reference (`private-key-signer.ts`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1917:+38 lines. Holds `Uint8Array` key, derives pubkey on demand, signs via nostr-tools `finalizeEvent`, encrypts via `nip04`/`nip44.v2`. `fromKey(privateKey: Uint8Array | string)` normalizes hex / nsec via `normalizeToSecretKey` (line 33-37). Nothing surprising — this is the shape every other signer should reduce to.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2493:+      ├─ signers (nip07, nip46, private-key)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2741:+### `NDKPrivateKeySigner` (`core/src/signers/private-key/index.ts`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2744:+- Constructor accepts hex private key, `Uint8Array`, or `nsec1...` bech32 (auto-decoded — explicit guardrail in JSDoc against pre-decoding).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3453:    33	- **App-extension kernel boundary** (ADR-0009). NMP is a kernel + five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`). Per-app concrete enums generated at the FFI boundary via `nmp gen modules` (ADR-0010). Apps assemble themselves from `nmp-core` + protocol modules + their own `<app>-core` crate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3455:    35	- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3660:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3670:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3688:     9	> **Status:** Draft 0, revised for ADR-0009 and ADR-0010. The kernel/module split is now architectural ground truth; product modules still graduate by the phased plan in `docs/plan.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3710:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3712:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3741:    62	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3757:     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (the resolution algorithm — read & write rows); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` (the `PublishPlanner` trait); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3 (the read-side compiler pipeline); `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.5 (privacy-sensitive routes fail closed).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3759:     6	Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3783:    30	## C7. Write fan-out: outbox + recipient-inbox; private events fail closed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3785:    32	**Statement.** Every publish action's signed event is routed by the `PublishPlanner` (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1) according to a `PublishPrivacy` mode the action declares. **Public** events go to author write relays. **PublicWithNotifications** events go to author writes ∪ recipient inboxes (`#p` tagged pubkeys). **PrivateToRecipients** events (gift-wrapped per NIP-59) go to **only** resolved recipient inbox relays — never the author's writes, never the active session's defaults, never the indexer set. If any recipient has no declared inbox, the publish fails closed with `PublishPlanError::PrivateRecipientUnroutable`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3790:    37	- Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` check on recipient inbox lookups is the structural fail-closed for private events.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3793:    40	**App writes:** nothing — for the publish path. The app dispatches a publish action (`SendNote`, `React`, `SendDm`, etc.); the action's privacy mode is determined by the action type, not by an app-supplied parameter. There is no `relays` field on `SendNote`. The override exists for tests, migrations, and operator power-user flows; it is structurally outside the safe app path.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3795:    42	**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3797:    44	**Test:** `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`. The test has three sub-paths:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3800:    47	2. **PublicWithNotifications:** dispatch a note tagging Bob (Bob has one inbox relay seeded); assert the plan is Alice's writes ∪ Bob's inbox, with the correct `PublishRouteReason::AuthorWriteRelay` / `RecipientInbox` tagging per assignment.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3802:    49	4. **Override rejection:** dispatch a `PublishWithOverride` carrying a `PrivateToRecipients` inner action and an override relay set that includes a non-inbox URL; assert it rejects with `PublishPlanError::OverrideRejected { reason: "private widen" }` (rule 4 of `outbox.md` §7.4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3986:    38	- DM-private events refuse non-inbox relays (M9 — deferred but the structural ban stays in M2 outbox planner)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3999:    51	1. **Applesauce explorer** — full deep-dive on `/private/tmp/nostr-research/applesauce` (git-tracked, May 2026). Focus: outbox impl, event-store query builders, subscription auto-tracking on kind:3, signer flows, missing features beneficial for NMP. Include git-log archaeology for known gotchas.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4008:    60	- DMs deferred ≠ no privacy at v1. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays (M2) stays. When DMs land post-v1, they slot in cleanly because the routing contract is already there.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4048:README.md:35:- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4067:    62	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4130:   127	The framework implements the outbox model **by default and automatically**. Subscriptions with `authors` filters automatically route reads to those authors' write relays. Publishes for an event automatically go to the author's write relays plus inbox relays of any `p`-tagged recipients (for DMs and notifications). The developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does. They can override, but the override is the exception.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4142:   139	Account persistence is automatic via the OS keyring crate (Keychain / Credential Manager / Secret Service). Signers cover the spectrum: private key (encrypted at rest), NIP-49 password-encrypted, NIP-07 browser extension (with a proxy for native apps), NIP-46 bunker (Nostr Connect), and platform-native external signers (e.g. the Android Amber app via NIP-55, a capability bridge in RMP terms).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4158:   155	A **conversation layer** wraps NIP-17 private DMs (gift-wrapped via NIP-59, encrypted via NIP-44) into a conversation-list and message-list view. Sending a DM is an action; receiving DMs is a subscription. Decryption happens in the Rust core, never in platform code. On mobile this includes background-decryption via a platform-specific notification service extension that calls into the Rust core.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4333:    81	| Subscription with `p` tag filter or notifications | Union of each tagged pubkey's inbox relays. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4336:    84	| Publish with `p` tags (DMs, mentions, reactions) | Author's write relays **plus** each tagged pubkey's inbox relays. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4337:    85	| DM (NIP-17 gift-wrapped) | **Only** resolved recipient inbox relays. Never the author's write relays. Never the active session's "default" relays. Missing recipient inbox relays fail closed. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4343:    91	- "DM accidentally public" → ruled out by the safe API. The DM publish path consults only resolved inbox relays; there is no fallback-to-all-relays path for gift wraps.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4382:   130	- NIP-49 (password-encrypted private key)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5139:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (the resolution algorithm — read & write rows); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` (the `PublishPlanner` trait); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3 (the read-side compiler pipeline); `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.5 (privacy-sensitive routes fail closed).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5143:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:32:**Statement.** Every publish action's signed event is routed by the `PublishPlanner` (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1) according to a `PublishPrivacy` mode the action declares. **Public** events go to author write relays. **PublicWithNotifications** events go to author writes ∪ recipient inboxes (`#p` tagged pubkeys). **PrivateToRecipients** events (gift-wrapped per NIP-59) go to **only** resolved recipient inbox relays — never the author's writes, never the active session's defaults, never the indexer set. If any recipient has no declared inbox, the publish fails closed with `PublishPlanError::PrivateRecipientUnroutable`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:80:`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
docs/design/view-catalog/template-and-enumeration.md:9:> **Status:** Rev 2, reframed per ADR-0009. These view kinds are not in `nmp-core`; apps consume them by adding the owning module crate to `nmp.toml` and regenerating the per-app FFI crate.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:29:| `DomainModule` rows (drafts, settings, action ledger, projection caches) | Entirely out of scope; the crate stores Nostr `Event` only | ADR-0009 + `kernel-substrate.md` §2 — kernel hosts non-Nostr typed rows |
docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:15:Parsed kind:10002 for one pubkey. Payload: `MailboxesView { pubkey, inbox_relays: Vec<String>, outbox_relays: Vec<String>, raw_event_id }`. Phase 1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:18:- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:57:✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:5:> **Status:** rev 1. Adopted alongside ADR-0009 and ADR-0010.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:7:> **Prerequisites:** `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md` (the architectural proposal), ADR-0009 (kernel boundary), ADR-0010 (generated app enum), `docs/design/reactivity.md` (the reactive machinery these modules plug into), `docs/design/view-catalog.md` (reference view modules).
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:391:- **Capabilities are module-private.** A capability registered by Module A is callable only by code that has a typed handle to it. The kernel does not route across.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:50:    /// hints if they conflict with policy (e.g. private-publish privacy).

docs/design/firehose-bench.md:238:**Setup.** Live mode, 10 relays, account following 500 pubkeys, scenario alternating between: 5min following timeline, 2min hashtag firehose, 1min DM inbox, 30s profile views, 1min idle.

Indexer-sourced inbox means we have no NIP-65-declared inbox; for private events that is fail-closed. The recipient gets nothing rather than getting a public broadcast.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1236:+No tokio. The actor's runtime is owned by `nmp-core`; action modules return state machines, not async tasks (see ADR-0009).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1827:+The reference Swift app uses `import FoundationModels` and `LanguageModelSession()`. That is Apple Intelligence on-device — free, private, requires no API key, but **iOS-only and capped to Apple-managed models**. To preserve UX parity on iPhone (no API key onboarding, no network for inference), the rebuild must route through Apple Intelligence when available.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1979:+`podcast-core` owns the action **shape**; the LLM call is delegated to `podcast-llm` via a private `ctx.dispatch_internal(SummarizePrompt { episode_id, style })` (one actor message tick — no FFI boundary crossed). This avoids `podcast-core` depending on `podcast-llm` while keeping the action namespaced under `podcast.*` from the Swift app's POV.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2179:+- **Free / offline / private** — matches the Swift app's no-API-key onboarding.

docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (the resolution algorithm — read & write rows); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` (the `PublishPlanner` trait); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3 (the read-side compiler pipeline); `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.5 (privacy-sensitive routes fail closed).
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:30:## C7. Write fan-out: outbox + recipient-inbox; private events fail closed
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:32:**Statement.** Every publish action's signed event is routed by the `PublishPlanner` (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1) according to a `PublishPrivacy` mode the action declares. **Public** events go to author write relays. **PublicWithNotifications** events go to author writes ∪ recipient inboxes (`#p` tagged pubkeys). **PrivateToRecipients** events (gift-wrapped per NIP-59) go to **only** resolved recipient inbox relays — never the author's writes, never the active session's defaults, never the indexer set. If any recipient has no declared inbox, the publish fails closed with `PublishPlanError::PrivateRecipientUnroutable`.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:37:- Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` check on recipient inbox lookups is the structural fail-closed for private events.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:40:**App writes:** nothing — for the publish path. The app dispatches a publish action (`SendNote`, `React`, `SendDm`, etc.); the action's privacy mode is determined by the action type, not by an app-supplied parameter. There is no `relays` field on `SendNote`. The override exists for tests, migrations, and operator power-user flows; it is structurally outside the safe app path.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:42:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:44:**Test:** `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`. The test has three sub-paths:
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:47:2. **PublicWithNotifications:** dispatch a note tagging Bob (Bob has one inbox relay seeded); assert the plan is Alice's writes ∪ Bob's inbox, with the correct `PublishRouteReason::AuthorWriteRelay` / `RecipientInbox` tagging per assignment.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:49:4. **Override rejection:** dispatch a `PublishWithOverride` carrying a `PrivateToRecipients` inner action and an override relay set that includes a non-inbox URL; assert it rejects with `PublishPlanError::OverrideRejected { reason: "private widen" }` (rule 4 of `outbox.md` §7.4).
docs/research/ndk/signers.md:31:### `NDKPrivateKeySigner` (`core/src/signers/private-key/index.ts`)
docs/research/ndk/signers.md:34:- Constructor accepts hex private key, `Uint8Array`, or `nsec1...` bech32 (auto-decoded — explicit guardrail in JSDoc against pre-decoding).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:174:++4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:175:++5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:263:++`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:626:++    /// hints if they conflict with policy (e.g. private-publish privacy).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:876:++pub fn resolve_author_inbox(

This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:915:++- Future `nmp-nip17` (M9) — DM publish path imports `resolve_author_inbox` for recipient lookups.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1074:++- **Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` check** is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #4 (`docs/plan.md` line 306 — "DM to public: no API path can send a DM to a non-inbox relay"). Indexer-sourced inbox means we have no NIP-65-declared inbox; for private events that is fail-closed. The recipient gets nothing rather than getting a public broadcast.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1084:++4. **Refused for privacy-sensitive modes** — `PublishPrivacy::PrivateToRecipients` rejects an override that adds non-inbox relays. The override may *narrow* a private fan-out to a subset of declared inboxes; it may not *widen* to public relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1125:++        // ... validate that inner action's privacy mode permits override ...
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1274:++Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1312:++M6+ trigger. Some interests (private DMs in M9, NIP-42 challenge response in M5) only become routable once a signer is loaded for their account. M2 records the trigger shape; behaviour is no-op pre-M6.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1316:++The actor's planner inbox is a FIFO queue. Order matters only at the granularity of a tick: within a tick, all queued triggers are folded into the compile inputs and one compile runs. Across ticks, recompiles happen in order received.
