docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1776:+docs/aim.md:219:## 6. Doctrine — the rules the API must make hard to violate
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1783:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1787:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1788:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1794:+docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1795:+docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1798:+docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1799:+docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1800:+docs/plan.md:100:- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1818:+docs/plan.md:271:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1820:+docs/plan.md:335:- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1821:+docs/plan.md:356:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1823:+docs/plan.md:380:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2075:+docs/design/view-catalog/template-and-enumeration.md:17:Every reference Nostr view module lives in a `nmp-nip*` crate and implements `ViewModule` from `nmp-core::substrate`:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2102:+docs/decisions/0010-generated-app-enum-vs-type-erased-registry.md:184:- Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) generates a tiny `nmp-app-fixture` crate with one module set demonstrating DomainModule, ViewModule, ActionModule, CapabilityModule, and IdentityModule. Bindings compile and round-trip a dispatch in the reference shell.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2105:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:19:The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2107:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2108:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:33:Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2111:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2122:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:180:| `nmp-core` | Kernel substrate: actor, store, planner, ledger, registries, extension traits, diagnostics | Pure Rust |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2134:+docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:5:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 (the slice is now built on top of the kernel substrate; the kind:0 path is a Profile `ViewModule` in `nmp-nip01`, not a built-in feature of `nmp-core`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2156:+docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2171:+     4	> Read first: `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) for the trait this crate implements; `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md` §3 layering table — `nmp-nip65` is a **protocol module**, not an app module.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2173:+     6	`nmp-nip65` is the first NMP protocol module (per the v1 reference-modules list in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §11) whose job is *not* to expose product views. It exists primarily as **the canonical source of the mailbox cache** the compiler consults. It contributes one `ViewModule` (for app-side rendering of "this user's relay list") and a small public API the compiler imports directly without going through the FFI surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2357:+   190	## 6.5 Module composition (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §8)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2361:+   194	- `nmp-core::substrate::{ViewModule, ViewContext, InterestContext, LogicalInterest, ...}` — kernel trait surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2394:+     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2466:+    76	The trait is consumed by the action ledger (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 — the kernel owns "per-relay publish attempts" provenance). When an `ActionModule::reduce` reaches its publish step, it calls the planner, gets a `PublishPlan`, and the kernel fans out to relays with the standard ledger-correlated retry/cancel semantics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2522:+   132	- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2528:+   138	The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2600:+   210	Per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 ("Atomicity"): the action ledger ensures the action's local store insert (for the signed event) happens in the same actor message as the ledger transition. The publish plan's per-relay attempts are *not* atomic with the local insert — relays may NACK over a long window — but the ledger correlates them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2604:+   214	- The publish-fanout step in `PublishWithOverride::reduce` is `AwaitCapability { request: CapabilityRequest::Publish { ... }, next_step }` per the `ActionTransition` enum in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4. The kernel owns the publish attempts and reports per-relay outcomes back into the next `reduce`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2605:+   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2609:+   219	- **Action ledger schema** — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 is the design; M6 implements.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2789:+   171	| `close_author` / `close_thread` (requests.rs:265-311) | Drop interests by `InterestId`; recompile with `Trigger::ViewClose`. Wire-emitter closes orphaned REQs. The "warm-close" grace from the view-warmth doctrine (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 "lifecycle") is the compiler's, not the view's — interests stay registered for the warmth window after their last claim. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:2825:+    10	Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 (lines 39–46) and `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("provenance preserved"), the four relay-fact lanes are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3024:+    15	- **External** triggers are `AppAction` variants the platform may dispatch directly. There is exactly one — `AppAction::InvalidateCompile { reason }` — to keep the public surface minimal per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3116:+   107	Emitted by the view registry after the warmth grace expires for an interest with refcount = 0. The warmth window is configurable (`AppConfig.view_warmth_ms`, default 30,000 — matching the doctrine in `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 "View warmth"). Closing an interest mid-warmth (e.g. account switch invalidates the prior account's interests) is a separate `ActiveAccountChanged` trigger, not this one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3389:+   219	This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3454:+     5	> **Companion docs:** `docs/plan.md` §M2; `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3509:+    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3511:+    17	- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3585:+    91	Every `ViewModule` (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3) declares its dependencies via the existing `ViewDependencies` mechanism. M2 adds one new method:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3600:+   106	`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3622:+   128	- A **publish**. Publishes are durable actions on the action ledger (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4); they consult the `PublishPlanner` (§7) but they are not interests because they do not stay alive.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3642:+    29	- Domain-store substrate for non-Nostr records.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3661:+    48	| `nmp-core` kernel | actor, store substrate, planner, ledger, registries, codegen, diagnostics | No |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3666:+    53	**Five extension trait families** (concrete signatures in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3700:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3948:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:132:- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:3950:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138:The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4360:+/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/kernel-substrate.md | sed -n '180,240p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4419:+   237	**Atomicity** (doctrine guarantee): the kernel ensures `module.reduce(...)`, ledger transitions, and local store writes happen as one actor message. External effects such as relay publishes cannot be rolled back after a relay accepts them, so publish steps must be ledger-correlated and restart-recoverable: a "publish accepted but local insert failed" path becomes an explicit failed/recovery ledger state, not silent divergence.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4431:+- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4433:+- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4441:+- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4489:+You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4531:++> **Prerequisites:** `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (insert invariants), `docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md` (GC policy intent), `docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md` (DomainModule storage), `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §2 (DomainModule trait).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4554:++| `DomainModule` rows (drafts, settings, action ledger, projection caches) | Entirely out of scope; the crate stores Nostr `Event` only | ADR-0009 + `kernel-substrate.md` §2 — kernel hosts non-Nostr typed rows |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4555:++| Migrations versioned per namespace | Out of scope | `kernel-substrate.md` §2: `DomainModule::migrations() -> Vec<DomainMigration>` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4600:++**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4638:++See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4. A `DomainModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §2) declares `const NAMESPACE: &'static str` and `const SCHEMA_VERSION: u32` plus `fn migrations() -> Vec<DomainMigration>`. The store assigns one LMDB sub-database per `(namespace, "data")`, plus one per `(namespace, index_name)` for each declared index. A `_meta` sub-database tracks `(namespace, current_version)`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4640:++The current `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41`) discards the concrete `M: DomainModule` type after `register_domain::<M>()` returns — only the `ModuleDescriptor` is retained. The store cannot get from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()` at runtime. M3 adds a `DomainFactories { schema_version: fn() -> u32, migrations: fn() -> Vec<DomainMigration>, indexes: fn() -> Vec<DomainIndex> }` struct attached per descriptor, populated by capturing the `M::*` consts and fns in `fn`-pointer closures at register time. This matches the existing `key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>` pattern in `DomainIndex` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:18`) — no `Box<dyn DomainModule>` and no new trait object-safety constraints on `DomainModule`. The change is additive to the substrate module surface. See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4.1 for the registry-side code shape.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4748:++6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4756:++- Substrate `DomainModule` trait the store backs: `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:1` (current shape, lines 1–49).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4757:++- Module registry the store consumes at startup: `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41` (`ModuleRegistry::register_domain`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:4894:++1. The view module's `dependencies(spec)` is consulted (per `kernel-substrate.md` §3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5077:++- `domain_foo.bar_idx_<index>` — one sub-db per `DomainIndex` (per `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:16`). Key = `index_key_fn(data_value) ‖ primary_key`; value = empty. The index is rewritten on every put (delete-old, write-new).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5079:++The actor exposes them only via `DomainHandle` (see [`trait.md`](trait.md) §4); modules never see the sub-db handles directly. Module isolation per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 is preserved: the handle factory checks the caller's registered namespace.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5286:++### 2.13 Domain isolation (`kernel-substrate.md` §8)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5600:++                      migrations: &[crate::substrate::DomainMigration])
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5631:++A handle is module-scoped; the kernel does not give a `DraftsModule` handle to `SettingsModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 "Domain stores are isolated"). The handle is `'env`-bounded so it cannot outlive the LMDB environment.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5633:++## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5639:++- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5770:++Per `kernel-substrate.md` §2: `DomainModule` declares `SCHEMA_VERSION` and `migrations()`. The store handles applying them at startup.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5774:++The existing `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:36-79`) stores only `ModuleDescriptor { namespace, family, rust_type }` — the concrete `M: DomainModule` type is consumed by the generic `register_domain::<M>()` call and not retained, so the store has no runtime path from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()`. M3 extends `ModuleDescriptor` for the Domain family with two `fn`-pointer factories — matching the existing `DomainIndex::key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> ...` pattern (`substrate/domain.rs:18`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5777:++// Added in M3 — substrate/domain.rs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5844:++`kernel-substrate.md` §12 already flags "cross-module migration coordination" as an open question. M3 implements the per-module path; cross-module coordination is deferred to a future module that needs it. The trait surface does not preclude adding it later: a future `Vec<NamespaceDependency>` field on `DomainMigration` would let the runner topologically order modules' migrations.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5862:+Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5894:+docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:404:- Doctrine review signed in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5900:+docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5901:+docs/plan.md:19:- **Kernel substrate** in `crates/nmp-core` (~3,800 LOC): actor on a dedicated OS thread, mailbox-driven (ADR feedback adopted — relay reads happen in tokio reader tasks, the actor blocks on its own channel with deadline timeouts), substrate trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`) in `nmp-core/src/substrate/`, ingest pipeline (`kernel/ingest.rs`), claim/release refcounting for profile interest (commit `23ae829`), composite reverse-index dependency tracking.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5902:+docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5903:+docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5905:+docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5906:+docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5907:+docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5922:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:15:You are reviewing merge 031fc07 (M2 subscription compilation + outbox routing design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5923:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:547:+- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5924:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1112:+- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5933:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1732:docs/aim.md:219:## 6. Doctrine — the rules the API must make hard to violate
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5934:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1739:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5935:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1743:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5936:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1744:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5937:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5938:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1751:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5939:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1754:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5940:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5958:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2063:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5959:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2067:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5963:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2112:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5964:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2561:   215	- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5965:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3465:    15	- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5966:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3656:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5968:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3732:docs/aim.md:219:## 6. Doctrine — the rules the API must make hard to violate
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5970:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3913:   219	## 6. Doctrine — the rules the API must make hard to violate
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5972:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5973:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4389:- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:5974:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4397:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6048:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1800:+You are reviewing a session's worth of merges on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. NMP is a Rust multiplatform framework for Nostr apps building toward v1 per docs/plan.md. Doctrine D0–D5 (docs/product-spec.md §1.5):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6049:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1801:+- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6050:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1802:+- D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6051:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1803:+- D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bound)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6052:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1804:+- D3 errors never cross FFI (become toast state fields)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6053:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1805:+- D4 one writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6054:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1806:+- D5 capabilities report, never decide
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6056:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1830:+- All 5 cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) compliant
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6057:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1839:+Doctrine compliance (exit gates for M10.5):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6058:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1840:+✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6059:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1841:+✅ D1 (best-effort rendering): ProfileCard.placeholder renders immediately
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6060:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1842:+✅ D2 (reactivity contract): all updates flow through composite reverse index
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6061:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1843:+✅ D3 (errors never cross FFI): errors as advisory JSON data, not FFI codes
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6062:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1844:+✅ D4 (one writer per fact): kernel actor is single-threaded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6063:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1845:+✅ D5 (capabilities report): iOS bridge is pure relay, no policy decisions

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6069:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2170:++### D0 Audit: Kernel Never Grows App Nouns

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6073:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2225:++### D4 Audit: One Writer Per Fact

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6080:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6082:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2397:++  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6298:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6299:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3991:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6300:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3992:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6301:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4005:     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6305:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4083:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6306:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4096:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6307:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4098:    75	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6329:+docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6330:+docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6342:+docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6363:+docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6364:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9fead0e (M3 LMDB schema + EventStore trait + GC policy design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5. File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6371:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:57:+> **Prerequisites:** `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (insert invariants), `docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md` (GC policy intent), `docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md` (DomainModule storage), `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §2 (DomainModule trait).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6386:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:126:+**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6394:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:274:+6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6416:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1159:+## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6417:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1165:+- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6425:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6473:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3720:    85	### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6475:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6476:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6477:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6478:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6503:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6504:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6505:+docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6543:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:535:+**Code Quality:** Zero bugs. All 20 code inspection findings are acceptable or justified by design. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6544:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6578:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6681:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:6:> **Prerequisites:** `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (insert invariants), `docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md` (GC policy intent), `docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md` (DomainModule storage), `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §2 (DomainModule trait).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6704:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:223:6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6708:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6709:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6710:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6711:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6712:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6713:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:75:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6714:+docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6716:+docs/design/podcast/wiring.md:88:- Acceptance: subscribe to a real RSS feed (Tim Ferriss) → library shows it → tap → see ≥ 5 episodes parsed; UI updates as `FetchFeed` action completes (no spinner gate; placeholder rows during fetch per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6721:+The first pass found the relevant doctrine anchors and the M3 docs. I’m also checking prior project memory for any known review pattern or prior M3 context, then I’ll reduce this to concrete findings.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6728:+2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6729:+2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6748:+    27	## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6752:+    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6754:+    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6762:+    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6766:+    45	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6775:+    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6781:+    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6794:+    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6796:+    75	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6798:+    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6870:+   179	Each payload type carries **pre-formatted** display strings (timestamps in user locale, npub-shortened forms, sat amounts). Per bible doctrine: no platform-side formatting.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6872:+   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6933:+   242	Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7149:+     5	> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7163:+    19	- **Kernel substrate** in `crates/nmp-core` (~3,800 LOC): actor on a dedicated OS thread, mailbox-driven (ADR feedback adopted — relay reads happen in tokio reader tasks, the actor blocks on its own channel with deadline timeouts), substrate trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`) in `nmp-core/src/substrate/`, ingest pipeline (`kernel/ingest.rs`), claim/release refcounting for profile interest (commit `23ae829`), composite reverse-index dependency tracking.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7164:+    20	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7165:+    21	- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7209:+    65	4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7210:+    66	5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7219:+    75	### M0 — Kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture *(DONE)*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7221:+    77	**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7225:+    81	**Subsystem deliverables.** `nmp-core::substrate`, `nmp-codegen`, `fixture-todo-core`, `nmp-testing` harness skeletons.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7227:+    83	**Exit gate.** ✅ Fixture compiles and runs; codegen determinism test passes; substrate registry test passes (`crates/nmp-core/tests/substrate_registry.rs`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7244:+   100	- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7329:+     6	> **Prerequisites:** `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (insert invariants), `docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md` (GC policy intent), `docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md` (DomainModule storage), `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §2 (DomainModule trait).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7352:+    29	| `DomainModule` rows (drafts, settings, action ledger, projection caches) | Entirely out of scope; the crate stores Nostr `Event` only | ADR-0009 + `kernel-substrate.md` §2 — kernel hosts non-Nostr typed rows |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7353:+    30	| Migrations versioned per namespace | Out of scope | `kernel-substrate.md` §2: `DomainModule::migrations() -> Vec<DomainMigration>` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7436:+   113	See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4. A `DomainModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §2) declares `const NAMESPACE: &'static str` and `const SCHEMA_VERSION: u32` plus `fn migrations() -> Vec<DomainMigration>`. The store assigns one LMDB sub-database per `(namespace, "data")`, plus one per `(namespace, index_name)` for each declared index. A `_meta` sub-database tracks `(namespace, current_version)`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7438:+   115	The current `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41`) discards the concrete `M: DomainModule` type after `register_domain::<M>()` returns — only the `ModuleDescriptor` is retained. The store cannot get from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()` at runtime. M3 adds a `DomainFactories { schema_version: fn() -> u32, migrations: fn() -> Vec<DomainMigration>, indexes: fn() -> Vec<DomainIndex> }` struct attached per descriptor, populated by capturing the `M::*` consts and fns in `fn`-pointer closures at register time. This matches the existing `key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>` pattern in `DomainIndex` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:18`) — no `Box<dyn DomainModule>` and no new trait object-safety constraints on `DomainModule`. The change is additive to the substrate module surface. See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4.1 for the registry-side code shape.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7546:+   223	6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7554:+   231	- Substrate `DomainModule` trait the store backs: `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:1` (current shape, lines 1–49).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7555:+   232	- Module registry the store consumes at startup: `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41` (`ModuleRegistry::register_domain`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7571:+   219	## 6. Doctrine — the rules the API must make hard to violate
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7600:+A few likely consistency issues are emerging: key-layout drift between the master doc and `keys.md`, trait methods named in summaries but absent from the actual trait, and a possible D2/working-set leak around the “open view cover” being outside the trait contract. I’m checking those against the exact sub-doc lines now.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7852:+   249	                      migrations: &[crate::substrate::DomainMigration])
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7883:+   280	A handle is module-scoped; the kernel does not give a `DraftsModule` handle to `SettingsModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 "Domain stores are isolated"). The handle is `'env`-bounded so it cannot outlive the LMDB environment.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7885:+   282	## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7891:+   288	- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8030:+   111	- `domain_foo.bar_idx_<index>` — one sub-db per `DomainIndex` (per `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:16`). Key = `index_key_fn(data_value) ‖ primary_key`; value = empty. The index is rewritten on every put (delete-old, write-new).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8032:+   113	The actor exposes them only via `DomainHandle` (see [`trait.md`](trait.md) §4); modules never see the sub-db handles directly. Module isolation per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 is preserved: the handle factory checks the caller's registered namespace.

docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:7:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 — the Twitter clone is now built as **the first canonical extension module set** (`twitter-core` app crate on top of `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / `nmp-nip10` / `nmp-nip25` protocol modules), not as a built-in feature of `nmp-core`. Sub-phase plan adjusted: 1a.0 (foundations) is followed by 1a.1 (**kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture module**) BEFORE 1a.2 (first Nostr protocol module + desktop avatar slice). Twitter clone features layer on from 1a.4 onward.
docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:94:**Exit gate.** Cold launch with primed LMDB renders the seed-driven timeline in ≤ 1.5s, showing fresh content from hundreds of authors followed by the seeds; tap an author → profile screen → back works; kind:0 arriving mid-scroll updates all author rows in place per doctrine D1; updating one seed's kind:3 mid-session re-resolves the timeline's author set without manual intervention; reactivity-bench `--standard` continues to pass at the larger author-set size; firehose-bench `live` for `sustained_firehose` (running at the real seed-author scale, not modeled) lands within budgets.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:17:- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:91:Every `ViewModule` (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3) declares its dependencies via the existing `ViewDependencies` mechanism. M2 adds one new method:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:106:`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:128:- A **publish**. Publishes are durable actions on the action ledger (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4); they consult the `PublishPlanner` (§7) but they are not interests because they do not stay alive.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:15:- **External** triggers are `AppAction` variants the platform may dispatch directly. There is exactly one — `AppAction::InvalidateCompile { reason }` — to keep the public surface minimal per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:107:Emitted by the view registry after the warmth grace expires for an interest with refcount = 0. The warmth window is configurable (`AppConfig.view_warmth_ms`, default 30,000 — matching the doctrine in `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 "View warmth"). Closing an interest mid-warmth (e.g. account switch invalidates the prior account's interests) is a separate `ActiveAccountChanged` trigger, not this one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:15:You are reviewing merge f1e374b (perf(m1): clean-build verification baseline) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what's open; AppState carries view payloads only for open views
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:104:+substrate_registry: 2 passed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:179:+- Simulator chosen: **iPhone 17 / iOS 26.5** (UUID `C380BA01-AD22-4F4A-8524-A309DA15144C`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:239:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget, (4) internal consistency. Be terse. If everything is fine, say so. If there is a real concern, name the file:line and what to fix.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:271:The commit only adds the perf baseline doc and screenshot; no substrate/app code is touched. I’m checking the doc text for stale TODO-style debt and contradictory claims now.
