docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:483:No TODO/FIXME-style markers were present in `docs/perf/m1`. I found a couple of doc-consistency issues worth separating from doctrine, which looks unaffected because this commit is docs-only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:489:3f5a2b3 docs(readme): align D0-D5 with canonical product-spec; log doctrine collision (PD-001)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:506:139:- Simulator chosen: **iPhone 17 / iOS 26.5** (UUID `C380BA01-AD22-4F4A-8524-A309DA15144C`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:531:* 3f5a2b3 docs(readme): align D0-D5 with canonical product-spec; log doctrine collision (PD-001)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:5:> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:7:> **Scope:** Enumerate every behavior the framework guarantees so the application does not have to author code for it. The user directive is explicit: *"apps shouldn't have to care or know about these operations happening in the background, things should just work."* This document is the contract; the test suite is the proof; the milestone implementations are the substrate.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:13:- [Intro — purpose, doctrine alignment, per-bullet template, how this contract evolves](framework-magic/intro.md)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:26:Each row binds a behavior to: the sub-file that specifies it, the test name in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`, the milestone that owns the implementation, and the doctrine clause it discharges.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:28:| # | Behavior | Sub-file | Test name | Milestone | Doctrine / spec |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:34:| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:38:| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:92:- This document does **not** describe the proof app, the starter app, or the kernel-substrate trait families — those are the substrate the contract holds the framework to.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:6:> **Prerequisites:** [`docs/plan/m11-podcast.md`](../plan/m11-podcast.md), `docs/aim.md`, `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md`, `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md`, `docs/decisions/0009`, `docs/decisions/0010`, `crates/fixture-todo-core/src/lib.rs`.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:13:A **pixel-perfect rebuild** of the canonical Swift podcast app at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (8,793 LOC across 47 Swift files, see [`inventory.md`](podcast/inventory.md)) running on NMP. Every Swift view file in `PodcastApp/Views/` is copied verbatim into `ios/NmpPodcast/Views/`; only the data source is rewired. All business logic — services, models, view models, the LLM/RAG/transcription pipeline, audio orchestration, downloads, RSS, recommendations — moves into Rust extension modules behind the kernel substrate (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`).
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:108:Plus seven new capability families landed in `nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/` (each pure-trait; impls live in the platform shell). See [`podcast/capabilities.md`](podcast/capabilities.md).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:76:The trait is consumed by the action ledger (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 — the kernel owns "per-relay publish attempts" provenance). When an `ActionModule::reduce` reaches its publish step, it calls the planner, gets a `PublishPlan`, and the kernel fans out to relays with the standard ledger-correlated retry/cancel semantics.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:132:- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138:The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:210:Per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 ("Atomicity"): the action ledger ensures the action's local store insert (for the signed event) happens in the same actor message as the ledger transition. The publish plan's per-relay attempts are *not* atomic with the local insert — relays may NACK over a long window — but the ledger correlates them.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:214:- The publish-fanout step in `PublishWithOverride::reduce` is `AwaitCapability { request: CapabilityRequest::Publish { ... }, next_step }` per the `ActionTransition` enum in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4. The kernel owns the publish attempts and reports per-relay outcomes back into the next `reduce`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:219:- **Action ledger schema** — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 is the design; M6 implements.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:179:Each payload type carries **pre-formatted** display strings (timestamps in user locale, npub-shortened forms, sat amounts). Per bible doctrine: no platform-side formatting.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:443:| Future module | v1 substrate it must consume |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:171:| `close_author` / `close_thread` (requests.rs:265-311) | Drop interests by `InterestId`; recompile with `Trigger::ViewClose`. Wire-emitter closes orphaned REQs. The "warm-close" grace from the view-warmth doctrine (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 "lifecycle") is the compiler's, not the view's — interests stay registered for the warmth window after their last claim. |
docs/plan/m0-fixture.md:1:# M0 — Kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture *(DONE)*
docs/plan/m0-fixture.md:3:> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Arc 1 — Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack.
docs/plan/m0-fixture.md:5:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/plan/m0-fixture.md:9:**Subsystem deliverables.** `nmp-core::substrate`, `nmp-codegen`, `fixture-todo-core`, `nmp-testing` harness skeletons.
docs/plan/m0-fixture.md:11:**Exit gate.** ✅ Fixture compiles and runs; codegen determinism test passes; substrate registry test passes (`crates/nmp-core/tests/substrate_registry.rs`).
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:34:- domain store substrate for non-Nostr records,
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:77:NMP should expose a namespaced domain-store substrate:
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:262:If multiple apps repeat a pattern, extract the deterministic substrate, not the product noun. For example:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:219:This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:15:You are reviewing merge e9cbafa on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what's open; AppState carries view payloads only for open views
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:45:+> **Three arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:59:+- **Kernel substrate** in `crates/nmp-core` (~3,800 LOC): actor on a dedicated OS thread, mailbox-driven (ADR feedback adopted — relay reads happen in tokio reader tasks, the actor blocks on its own channel with deadline timeouts), substrate trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`) in `nmp-core/src/substrate/`, ingest pipeline (`kernel/ingest.rs`), claim/release refcounting for profile interest (commit `23ae829`), composite reverse-index dependency tracking.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:60:+- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:61:+- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:104:+4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:105:+5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:114:+### M0 — Kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture *(DONE)*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:117:+**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:123:+**Subsystem deliverables.** `nmp-core::substrate`, `nmp-codegen`, `fixture-todo-core`, `nmp-testing` harness skeletons.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:125:-5. **The doctrine list (D1–D5) is the rubric.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines. If a change makes any doctrine harder to enforce, it gets rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:126:+**Exit gate.** ✅ Fixture compiles and runs; codegen determinism test passes; substrate registry test passes (`crates/nmp-core/tests/substrate_registry.rs`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:146:+- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:230:-### Phase 1 — Kernel substrate + Twitter demo on iOS
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:233:-Per ADR-0006 (slice discipline), ADR-0008 (Twitter clone iOS target), ADR-0009 (kernel + extension modules), and ADR-0010 (generated app enum), Phase 1 grows the kernel substrate first, then layers Nostr protocol modules and the Twitter clone on top. Every sub-phase has running code at its exit gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:240:-- **1a.1** **Kernel substrate prototype + non-Nostr fixture** — the five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`); composite reverse index; delta buffer with coalescing; claim-based GC; codegen producing a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate from a `fixture-todo-core` app module that implements all five trait families; desktop iced shell rendering a TODO list. ~2 weeks. **Proves: kernel substrate works for non-Nostr-shaped data; codegen pipeline functional.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:241:-- **1a.2** First Nostr protocol module + desktop avatar slice — `nmp-nip01` (Event, Filter, Keys, Profile `ViewModule`); `useProfile` wrapper; primal connection via `nostr-sdk`; desktop iced avatar demo. The original ADR-0006 slice, now built on the kernel substrate from 1a.1. ~1 week.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:391:+**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:452:-- Best-effort doctrine enforced: timeline view renders posts whose authors have no kind:0 yet; placeholders are shown; when kind:0 arrives, in-place update.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:505:+- Profile picture update through compose → kind:0 republish with new Blossom URL → in-place refinement across all open Profile / Timeline payloads (per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:808:-The cross-platform consistency tests are the highest-value layer: they catch every drift between platforms and force the doctrine (Rust owns everything but rendering) to remain real.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:814:+| **Non-Nostr data bridge** | M0 (substrate), M10 (long-running capabilities), M11 (podcast app proves it in production) | DomainModule trait + ADR-0007 bridge lanes; first proven by fixture-todo-core; production proof in podcast app. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:880:-3. View payloads are non-optional with placeholders (D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:897:-- **ADR-0009:** App extension kernel boundary. NMP is reframed as a Nostr-native app kernel with five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`). The kernel owns substrate; protocol modules and app crates own nouns. Closed enums in the API are replaced by per-app generated enums (see ADR-0010). Phase 1a restructured to build the kernel substrate (with a non-Nostr fixture module) before the first Nostr protocol module. Adopted 2026-05-17 from `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:904:+| Unit | `cargo test` per crate | Pure-function correctness, substrate trait invariants, codegen determinism | Each crate's `tests/` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:957:+- **ADR-0006**: Vertical-slice-first delivery (modified by ADR-0009; the slice now layers on the kernel substrate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:1008:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget, (4) internal consistency with the plan's own stated scope. Be terse. If everything is fine, say so. If there is a real concern, name the file:line and what to fix.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:1010:I’ll verify the checked-out `docs/plan.md` directly for line numbers, LOC, and any TODO-style markers, then cross-check the changed plan against the doctrines and its own stated milestone scope.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:475:docs/design/podcast/inventory.md:65:| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:482:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:484:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:106:- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:485:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:5:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:486:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:489:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:378:### D.8 Atomicity contract (per [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §4)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:491:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:392:Per ADR-0001, projection cache changes feed `ProjectionChange` events into `ViewModule::on_projection_changed`. Each is registered as a `ProjectionCache` in `nmp-core::substrate::projections` (kernel-owned trait; modules opt in).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:500:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:4:> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:504:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:85:### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:509:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:106:- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:516:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:519:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8302cc1.md:559:* d7af61b (origin/claude/review-rmp-spec-8a7VX, origin/HEAD, worktree-agent-a67512ccd903fbc26, claude/review-rmp-spec-8a7VX) docs: promote negentropy + best-effort rendering to cardinal doctrines; add build plan
docs/design/ffi-hardening/scenarios-detail.md:152:doctrine sign-off evidence for M10.5 (see gates.md §D1 note).
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:5:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 (the slice is now built on top of the kernel substrate; the kind:0 path is a Profile `ViewModule` in `nmp-nip01`, not a built-in feature of `nmp-core`)
docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/product-spec/appendices.md:31:| **Reactive shared SQLite.** Rust writes; both sides hold read handles; reactive query libraries (GRDB / SQLDelight / Drift) re-run queries on table writes. | 1Password (Op core), Linear, Notion mobile, most local-first apps | Surrenders doctrine. Platforms now write queries, which is display-shaping logic. Pre-formatting (timestamps, npubs, sats) either moves into native (D-violation) or materializes as columns at write time (extra schema). Web fragments — wasm SQLite doesn't share with JS the way native does. Cross-platform consistency tests get harder (per-platform query results vs byte-diffable JSON). |
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:18:  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:42:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:121:NMP's best-effort rendering doctrine still applies. Missing relay metadata should not block rendering cached data or placeholders. The system can start with known data, report degraded coverage, discover better relay metadata, and refine the view in place.
docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:157:- From NMP's own doctrine, keep Rust as the single owner of policy and keep platform code out of protocol decisions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:17:You are reviewing a session's worth of merges on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. NMP is a Rust multiplatform framework for Nostr apps building toward v1 per docs/plan.md. Doctrine D0–D5 (docs/product-spec.md §1.5):

docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:572:-> **Three arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:573:+> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:4:> Read first: `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) for the trait this crate implements; `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md` §3 layering table — `nmp-nip65` is a **protocol module**, not an app module.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6:`nmp-nip65` is the first NMP protocol module (per the v1 reference-modules list in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §11) whose job is *not* to expose product views. It exists primarily as **the canonical source of the mailbox cache** the compiler consults. It contributes one `ViewModule` (for app-side rendering of "this user's relay list") and a small public API the compiler imports directly without going through the FFI surface.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:190:## 6.5 Module composition (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §8)
docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:194:- `nmp-core::substrate::{ViewModule, ViewContext, InterestContext, LogicalInterest, ...}` — kernel trait surface.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:15:You are reviewing merge 9eace68 (plan(scope): defer DMs (M9) + Wallet (M12); add M11.5 Highlighter; framework-magic contract) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what's open; AppState carries view payloads only for open views
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:49:+| **M12 — Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)** | **Deferred to post-v1.** Wallet is huge surface area (NWC, NIP-57, NIP-60, NIP-61) and not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:50:+| *(none)* | **New M11.5 — Highlighter rebuild + `nmp-nip29` crate.** Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app proves the boundary. Source: `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs; the rebuild port it onto NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern). Adds NIP-29 as a first-class protocol module crate (groups, moderation, joining flow, kind 39000–39003 metadata events) done in NMP-idiomatic shape. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:70:+A dedicated **doctrine document** + **kernel-substrate test suite** that enumerates every behavior the framework guarantees without the app authoring code for it. Working title: "Things that just work." Includes (non-exhaustive):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:78:+- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:292:docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:5:**Demo product:** A 1:1 rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs) running on NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern. Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app ([M11](m11-podcast.md)) proves the boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:311:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:115:The successor to the closed `ViewSpec` enum. Every view kind — kernel-Nostr (Profile, Timeline, Thread, ...), protocol-NIP (NIP-29 group state, NIP-17 conversation), or app-specific (Twitter compose, Highlighter capture queue) — is a `ViewModule`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:312:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:318:- `HighlighterOcrCapability` — OCR a clipboard image; return text.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:313:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:464:- `nmp-nip29` (groups), `nmp-nwc`, `nmp-cashu`, `nmp-nip57` (zaps), etc.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:314:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:465:- Highlighter-lite, TENEX-lite, etc. as demonstration extension modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:315:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:485:3. A future Highlighter-lite, TENEX-lite, or podcast-lite module can be added without changes to `nmp-core` traits. Demonstrated on paper for v1; demonstrated in code post-v1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:319:docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:324:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:10:| **M12 — Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)** | **Deferred to post-v1.** Wallet is huge surface area (NWC, NIP-57, NIP-60, NIP-61) and not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:325:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:11:| *(none)* | **New M11.5 — Highlighter rebuild + `nmp-nip29` crate.** Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app proves the boundary. Source: `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs; the rebuild port it onto NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern). Adds NIP-29 as a first-class protocol module crate (groups, moderation, joining flow, kind 39000–39003 metadata events) done in NMP-idiomatic shape. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:379:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:17:- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:380:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:91:Every `ViewModule` (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3) declares its dependencies via the existing `ViewDependencies` mechanism. M2 adds one new method:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:385:docs/design/framework-magic.md:13:- [Intro — purpose, doctrine alignment, per-bullet template, how this contract evolves](framework-magic/intro.md)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:397:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:400:docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:424:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:219:- **Action ledger schema** — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 is the design; M6 implements.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:446:docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4 (sessions + signer catalog); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §5 (`CapabilityModule`), §6 (`IdentityModule`); `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` §"Folded into M6".
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:460:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:45:**Milestone owner:** **[DONE]** for the placeholder shape (the M1 timeline slice already ships non-`Option` author fields with shortened-npub fallback — verified in `crates/nmp-core` timeline tests today). **[PENDING M2/M3]** for the full in-place refinement guarantees: sub-paths 1 and 5 are testable today; sub-paths 2 and 4 require the kernel's projection cache (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 148 `on_projection_changed`) which graduates in M2 alongside the view-module surface; sub-path 3 requires the per-tick re-format hook (`fn on_tick`, M2's `ViewModule` trait work).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:471:docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:16:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #1. Plus the doctrine-4 footgun: an app caches kind:3 in its own state, fails to re-fetch on UI nav, renders a stale follow list, double-subscribes on the next session.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:487:docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the canonical NDK-era bug: app code listens for kind:3 events, manually closes its open subscriptions, re-derives author lists, re-issues REQs, and either races itself (REQ ordering vs. local-state ordering) or leaks the old REQ. This contract structurally forbids that pattern: the view module never sees the kind:3 directly, and the app never issues a REQ. Specifically discharges aim.md §6 doctrine 6 ("subscriptions auto-group, auto-close, auto-dedup, auto-buffer; the developer never writes grouping/dedup/cleanup code") for the follow-list-change case.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:497:docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:55:`TBD-from-research(applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md)`: cite the query-builder API shape that lets a consumer phrase `"things kind:1 by people I follow"` once and get a stream that re-evaluates on every kind:3 change. Applesauce's mechanism is a builder that registers itself as a dependent of the kind:3 projection; the contract's `ViewModule.dependencies()` is the NMP analog (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 131–132). The research-fold commit cross-validates that the analog covers Applesauce's pattern fully.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:716:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:717:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:729:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2063:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:730:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2064:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:33:Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:837:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1622:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:272-282`: D0 is incorrectly marked compliant. The doc itself cites `Profile` and `TimelineItem` inside `nmp-core`, and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:57-114` contains `Profile`, `TimelineItem`, `ProfileCard`, `AuthorViewPayload`, and `ThreadViewPayload`. Per ADR-0009, `nmp-core` must not contain Profile/Timeline/Thread view-kind business logic. Fix the audit to mark this as blocking D0 debt or move those nouns out before signoff.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:838:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1633:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:272-282`: D0 is incorrectly marked compliant. The doc itself cites `Profile` and `TimelineItem` inside `nmp-core`, and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:57-114` contains `Profile`, `TimelineItem`, `ProfileCard`, `AuthorViewPayload`, and `ThreadViewPayload`. Per ADR-0009, `nmp-core` must not contain Profile/Timeline/Thread view-kind business logic. Fix the audit to mark this as blocking D0 debt or move those nouns out before signoff.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:849:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:406:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:854:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:442:+**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:860:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1060:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance — does the framework-magic contract violate D0 by adding app nouns to the kernel, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget per file, (4) internal consistency of the contract (are the 14 tests coherent with the contract). Be terse. Name file:line for any real concern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:861:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:862:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:105:+5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:864:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:233:-Per ADR-0006 (slice discipline), ADR-0008 (Twitter clone iOS target), ADR-0009 (kernel + extension modules), and ADR-0010 (generated app enum), Phase 1 grows the kernel substrate first, then layers Nostr protocol modules and the Twitter clone on top. Every sub-phase has running code at its exit gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:875:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:897:-- **ADR-0009:** App extension kernel boundary. NMP is reframed as a Nostr-native app kernel with five extension trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`). The kernel owns substrate; protocol modules and app crates own nouns. Closed enums in the API are replaced by per-app generated enums (see ADR-0010). Phase 1a restructured to build the kernel substrate (with a non-Nostr fixture module) before the first Nostr protocol module. Adopted 2026-05-17 from `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:876:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:957:+- **ADR-0006**: Vertical-slice-first delivery (modified by ADR-0009; the slice now layers on the kernel substrate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:879:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:1245:    62	5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:886:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:1601:   650	- **ADR-0006**: Vertical-slice-first delivery (modified by ADR-0009; the slice now layers on the kernel substrate).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:891:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:896:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:319:docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:903:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:397:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:909:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:534:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:919:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4054:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:929:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4056:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:949:docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:951:docs/decisions/0008-twitter-clone-on-ios-as-the-slice-target.md:7:**Modified by:** ADR-0009 — the Twitter clone is now built as **the first canonical extension module set** (`twitter-core` app crate on top of `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / `nmp-nip10` / `nmp-nip25` protocol modules), not as a built-in feature of `nmp-core`. Sub-phase plan adjusted: 1a.0 (foundations) is followed by 1a.1 (**kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture module**) BEFORE 1a.2 (first Nostr protocol module + desktop avatar slice). Twitter clone features layer on from 1a.4 onward.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1009:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1010:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1012:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1017:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:582:+**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.

C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1054:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3670:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1056:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3710:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1057:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3712:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1060:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3759:     6	Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1065:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3795:    42	**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1086:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:29:| `DomainModule` rows (drafts, settings, action ledger, projection caches) | Entirely out of scope; the crate stores Nostr `Event` only | ADR-0009 + `kernel-substrate.md` §2 — kernel hosts non-Nostr typed rows |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1088:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:18:- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1089:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:57:✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1090:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1091:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:5:> **Status:** rev 1. Adopted alongside ADR-0009 and ADR-0010.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1092:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:7:> **Prerequisites:** `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md` (the architectural proposal), ADR-0009 (kernel boundary), ADR-0010 (generated app enum), `docs/design/reactivity.md` (the reactive machinery these modules plug into), `docs/design/view-catalog.md` (reference view modules).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1093:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:391:- **Capabilities are module-private.** A capability registered by Module A is callable only by code that has a typed handle to it. The kernel does not route across.

The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1494:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2310:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1495:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2312:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1497:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2380:   247	| **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |

docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:112:| # | Scenario | Threading concern | Primary doctrine tested |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:114:| S1 | Mount/unmount churn | actor recv + refcount | D5 (snapshot bounded), bible #5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:116:| S3 | Snapshot pressure | listener serialization | bible #9 (≤60 Hz), D5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:117:| S4 | Reconciler back-pressure | listener channel growth | bible #9, D1 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:120:| S7 | Error-shape exhaustion | every invalid input path | D3 (no errors cross FFI) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:121:| S8 | Subscription planner DOS | OpenView/CloseView storm | D2 (≤60 Hz/view), D5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:122:| S9 | Relay flap | reconnect + watermark | bible #7, D2 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:176:├── doctrine-review.md       # D0–D5 sign-off (M10.5 exit-gate artifact)

docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:225:The debt-inventory's D3 audit (lines 317–334) concludes the same:
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:240:## 8. Doctrine review checklist
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:242:Full D0–D5 line-item-to-scenario mapping in
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:245:| Doctrine | Proven by |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:247:| **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:248:| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:249:| **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:250:| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:251:| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:252:| **D5** capabilities report, never decide | CapabilityModule code proof + S6 (relay capability lifecycle storms) |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:15:- **External** triggers are `AppAction` variants the platform may dispatch directly. There is exactly one — `AppAction::InvalidateCompile { reason }` — to keep the public surface minimal per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:107:Emitted by the view registry after the warmth grace expires for an interest with refcount = 0. The warmth window is configurable (`AppConfig.view_warmth_ms`, default 30,000 — matching the doctrine in `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 "View warmth"). Closing an interest mid-warmth (e.g. account switch invalidates the prior account's interests) is a separate `ActiveAccountChanged` trigger, not this one.
docs/plan/test-pyramid.md:7:| Unit | `cargo test` per crate | Pure-function correctness, substrate trait invariants, codegen determinism | Each crate's `tests/` |
docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:171:| `close_author` / `close_thread` (requests.rs:265-311) | Drop interests by `InterestId`; recompile with `Trigger::ViewClose`. Wire-emitter closes orphaned REQs. The "warm-close" grace from the view-warmth doctrine (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 "lifecycle") is the compiler's, not the view's — interests stay registered for the warmth window after their last claim. |
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:6:> **Prerequisites:** `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (insert invariants), `docs/decisions/0003-working-set-memory.md` (GC policy intent), `docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md` (DomainModule storage), `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §2 (DomainModule trait).
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:29:| `DomainModule` rows (drafts, settings, action ledger, projection caches) | Entirely out of scope; the crate stores Nostr `Event` only | ADR-0009 + `kernel-substrate.md` §2 — kernel hosts non-Nostr typed rows |
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:30:| Migrations versioned per namespace | Out of scope | `kernel-substrate.md` §2: `DomainModule::migrations() -> Vec<DomainMigration>` |
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:113:See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4. A `DomainModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §2) declares `const NAMESPACE: &'static str` and `const SCHEMA_VERSION: u32` plus `fn migrations() -> Vec<DomainMigration>`. The store assigns one LMDB sub-database per `(namespace, "data")`, plus one per `(namespace, index_name)` for each declared index. A `_meta` sub-database tracks `(namespace, current_version)`.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:115:The current `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41`) discards the concrete `M: DomainModule` type after `register_domain::<M>()` returns — only the `ModuleDescriptor` is retained. The store cannot get from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()` at runtime. M3 adds a `DomainFactories { schema_version: fn() -> u32, migrations: fn() -> Vec<DomainMigration>, indexes: fn() -> Vec<DomainIndex> }` struct attached per descriptor, populated by capturing the `M::*` consts and fns in `fn`-pointer closures at register time. This matches the existing `key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>` pattern in `DomainIndex` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:18`) — no `Box<dyn DomainModule>` and no new trait object-safety constraints on `DomainModule`. The change is additive to the substrate module surface. See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4.1 for the registry-side code shape.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:223:6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:231:- Substrate `DomainModule` trait the store backs: `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:1` (current shape, lines 1–49).
docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:232:- Module registry the store consumes at startup: `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41` (`ModuleRegistry::register_domain`).
docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:101:Per `kernel-substrate.md` §2: `DomainModule` declares `SCHEMA_VERSION` and `migrations()`. The store handles applying them at startup.
docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:105:The existing `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:36-79`) stores only `ModuleDescriptor { namespace, family, rust_type }` — the concrete `M: DomainModule` type is consumed by the generic `register_domain::<M>()` call and not retained, so the store has no runtime path from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()`. M3 extends `ModuleDescriptor` for the Domain family with two `fn`-pointer factories — matching the existing `DomainIndex::key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> ...` pattern (`substrate/domain.rs:18`):
docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:108:// Added in M3 — substrate/domain.rs
docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md:175:`kernel-substrate.md` §12 already flags "cross-module migration coordination" as an open question. M3 implements the per-module path; cross-module coordination is deferred to a future module that needs it. The trait surface does not preclude adding it later: a future `Vec<NamespaceDependency>` field on `DomainMigration` would let the runner topologically order modules' migrations.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:5:**Demo product:** A 1:1 rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs) running on NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern. Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app ([M11](m11-podcast.md)) proves the boundary.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:13:- Read `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` in full. Map every Swift/Kotlin view to its NMP extension-module equivalent. Identify what the existing Rust core already does vs what needs porting into the NMP substrate trait families.
docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:25:- `apps/highlighter/highlighter-core/` — DomainModules, ViewModules, ActionModules mirroring the existing Rust core in `hl/app` but wired into NMP substrate trait families.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:219:This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them.
docs/design/view-catalog/conversation-and-cross-cutting.md:95:- Decryption failure → don't add to view; record in `DebugDiagnostics`. Doctrine: never expose ciphertext as a message.
docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:249:                      migrations: &[crate::substrate::DomainMigration])
docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:280:A handle is module-scoped; the kernel does not give a `DraftsModule` handle to `SettingsModule` (per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 "Domain stores are isolated"). The handle is `'env`-bounded so it cannot outlive the LMDB environment.
docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:282:## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:288:- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:157:Doctrine:
docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:166:`AppUpdate` is the outbound stream. Bible doctrine: snapshots by default; granular variants only where profiling warrants. v1 starts with:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:17:- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:91:Every `ViewModule` (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3) declares its dependencies via the existing `ViewDependencies` mechanism. M2 adds one new method:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:106:`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:128:- A **publish**. Publishes are durable actions on the action ledger (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4); they consult the `PublishPlanner` (§7) but they are not interests because they do not stay alive.

docs/design/lmdb/keys.md:111:- `domain_foo.bar_idx_<index>` — one sub-db per `DomainIndex` (per `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:16`). Key = `index_key_fn(data_value) ‖ primary_key`; value = empty. The index is rewritten on every put (delete-old, write-new).
docs/design/lmdb/keys.md:113:The actor exposes them only via `DomainHandle` (see [`trait.md`](trait.md) §4); modules never see the sub-db handles directly. Module isolation per `kernel-substrate.md` §8 is preserved: the handle factory checks the caller's registered namespace.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:76:The trait is consumed by the action ledger (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 — the kernel owns "per-relay publish attempts" provenance). When an `ActionModule::reduce` reaches its publish step, it calls the planner, gets a `PublishPlan`, and the kernel fans out to relays with the standard ledger-correlated retry/cancel semantics.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:132:- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138:The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:210:Per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 ("Atomicity"): the action ledger ensures the action's local store insert (for the signed event) happens in the same actor message as the ledger transition. The publish plan's per-relay attempts are *not* atomic with the local insert — relays may NACK over a long window — but the ledger correlates them.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:214:- The publish-fanout step in `PublishWithOverride::reduce` is `AwaitCapability { request: CapabilityRequest::Publish { ... }, next_step }` per the `ActionTransition` enum in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4. The kernel owns the publish attempts and reports per-relay outcomes back into the next `reduce`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:219:- **Action ledger schema** — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 is the design; M6 implements.

docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:378:### D.8 Atomicity contract (per [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §4)
docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:392:Per ADR-0001, projection cache changes feed `ProjectionChange` events into `ViewModule::on_projection_changed`. Each is registered as a `ProjectionCache` in `nmp-core::substrate::projections` (kernel-owned trait; modules opt in).

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:179:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:10:| 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:192:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:193:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:194:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:195:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:11:### PD-001 — Doctrine vocabulary collision: D0–D5 vs RMP-bible invariants vs reactivity ADRs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:196:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:13:**Decision (autonomous):** The canonical D0–D5 is what's in `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (kernel-boundary, best-effort, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded). I corrected the README to match (commit pending in next push).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:197:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:15:**Why:** Codex review of merge `51120cb` flagged that I had been using a different D2/D3/D5 mapping throughout this session — conflating the product-spec D0–D5 with RMP-bible invariants (errors-never-cross-FFI is invariant #2; capabilities-report-don't-decide is RMP cardinal rule #6; ≤60 Hz reactivity is from ADR-0001..0004). They're all real binding rules, but they're three different rule sources. I had collapsed them into a single "D0–D5" rubric, which was wrong.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:198:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:19:- Various codex review prompts I wrote earlier passed the wrong D0–D5 to codex. The flagged findings in those reviews are still valid (codex review the doctrine word-by-word), but the framing was off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:199:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:20:- `docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md` — uses the canonical D0–D5 correctly (codex flagged + fixed earlier).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:200:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:22:**Open question for user:** Do you want me to (a) keep the canonical product-spec D0–D5 as the single rubric and downgrade the RMP-bible invariants + reactivity-ADR rules to "supporting rules" (cited by number where relevant); or (b) expand the canonical D0–D5 to incorporate the three additional load-bearing rules (would require editing `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 — that's the authoritative source so changing it is a meaningful commit).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:201:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:24:**Current default while you decide:** option (a). README + framework-magic docs will be aligned to product-spec D0–D5; supporting rules cited by their source (RMP-bible invariant N, ADR-NNNN, etc.).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:202:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:203:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:204:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:205:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:206:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:207:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:210:docs/design/view-catalog/stubs-validation-next.md:56:- **Computed-from-native fields.** All formatting, all derivations live in Rust per doctrine D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:211:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:103:- **Bare pubkey path.** If no kind:0 exists in the store yet, `open()` returns a placeholder-filled payload and the reverse-index dependency triggers `on_event_inserted` when one arrives. Doctrine D1 prohibits returning `None` here.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:212:docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md:234:- **Author kind:0 not yet loaded.** Per doctrine D1, render the item with placeholders. The `by_author` reindex on `on_projection_changed` updates the item in place. **The post is rendered immediately.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:213:docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:242:17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:214:docs/perf/m1/build-verification.md:139:- Simulator chosen: **iPhone 17 / iOS 26.5** (UUID `C380BA01-AD22-4F4A-8524-A309DA15144C`)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:215:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:280:- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:216:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:218:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:122:All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:219:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:220:docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:106:- The doctrine review at `docs/perf/m11/doctrine-review.md` signs off D0–D5 against the M11 surface (template: `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:224:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:19:   (`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D0–D5), and every ownership
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:225:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:226:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:114:| S1 | Mount/unmount churn | actor recv + refcount | D5 (snapshot bounded), bible #5 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:227:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:116:| S3 | Snapshot pressure | listener serialization | bible #9 (≤60 Hz), D5 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:228:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:117:| S4 | Reconciler back-pressure | listener channel growth | bible #9, D1 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:229:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:120:| S7 | Error-shape exhaustion | every invalid input path | D3 (no errors cross FFI) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:230:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:121:| S8 | Subscription planner DOS | OpenView/CloseView storm | D2 (≤60 Hz/view), D5 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:231:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:122:| S9 | Relay flap | reconnect + watermark | bible #7, D2 |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:288:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:578:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
