docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:379:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1800:+You are reviewing a session's worth of merges on master in the nostr-multi-platform repo. NMP is a Rust multiplatform framework for Nostr apps building toward v1 per docs/plan.md. Doctrine D0–D5 (docs/product-spec.md §1.5):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:380:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1801:+- D0 kernel never grows app nouns
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:381:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1802:+- D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:382:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1803:+- D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60 Hz/view, working-set bound)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:383:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1804:+- D3 errors never cross FFI (become toast state fields)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:384:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1805:+- D4 one writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:385:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1806:+- D5 capabilities report, never decide

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:387:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1840:+✅ D0 (kernel never grows app nouns): kernel is domain-agnostic
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:388:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1841:+✅ D1 (best-effort rendering): ProfileCard.placeholder renders immediately
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:389:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1842:+✅ D2 (reactivity contract): all updates flow through composite reverse index
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:390:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1843:+✅ D3 (errors never cross FFI): errors as advisory JSON data, not FFI codes

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:392:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1845:+✅ D5 (capabilities report): iOS bridge is pure relay, no policy decisions

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:403:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2361:++> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6544:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:576:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1848:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6546:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:612:+  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1856:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6578:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:215:- The local store insert happens *before* the publish step (optimistic insert), with rollback on `PartiallyFailed` if `required_success_count` is not met. This matches the "atomic with reversibility" reading of doctrine D4 (single writer per fact).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1857:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6581:+docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1858:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6582:+docs/design/podcast/exit-gate.md:85:Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1922:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6723:+/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "nostr-multi-platform|9fead0e|M3|lmdb|EventStore|Doctrine D0|D2|working-set|working set" /Users/pablofernandez/.codex/memories/MEMORY.md' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1923:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6725:+236:- com.podcastr.next.Podcastr, devicectl, 00008150-001E118E3CD2401C, Pablo's iPhone, xcodebuild -showdestinations, /tmp/podcast-rmp-add-toolbar-device-build, App installed, Launched application
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1924:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6726:+250:- the working device path here was `xcodebuild -showdestinations`, then `xcodebuild build` for device id `00008150-001E118E3CD2401C`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.podcastr.next.Podcastr` [Task 2]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1925:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6727:+2304:- `xcodegen generate` is required here after adding new Swift files, and the working device path was `xcodebuild -project WeightTracker.xcodeproj -scheme WeightTracker -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' build` followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch` [Task 4][Task 5]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1926:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6728:+2468:- the working iPhone path was `cargo build --target aarch64-apple-ios --release -p tenex-core`, then `xcodebuild -workspace TenexMVP.xcworkspace -scheme TenexMVP -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath .codex-derived-ios-device -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .codex-derived-ios-device/SourcePackages build`, followed by `xcrun devicectl device install app` and `xcrun devicectl device process launch --terminate-existing com.tenex.mvp` [Task 1]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1927:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6729:+2637:- the working iPhone deployment sequence was `xcodebuild ... -destination 'id=00008150-001E118E3CD2401C' -derivedDataPath /tmp/WinTheDayDerived build`, then `xcrun devicectl device install app`, then `xcrun devicectl device process launch`; `No provider was found` warnings from `devicectl` were noisy but non-fatal here [Task 3]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1928:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6752:+    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1929:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6762:+    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1930:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6775:+    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1931:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6781:+    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1932:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6794:+    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1933:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6798:+    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1934:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6872:+   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1935:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6933:+   242	Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1938:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7164:+    20	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1940:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7209:+    65	4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1941:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7210:+    66	5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1942:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7244:+   100	- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1946:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1947:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7600:+A few likely consistency issues are emerging: key-layout drift between the master doc and `keys.md`, trait methods named in summaries but absent from the actual trait, and a possible D2/working-set leak around the “open view cover” being outside the trait contract. I’m checking those against the exact sub-doc lines now.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1949:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7885:+   282	## 5. Error semantics (doctrine D3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1950:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7891:+   288	- `Encoding` → `tracing::error!` with the offending key/namespace; the action that triggered it fails with a `toast: Some("internal storage error; please restart")` per D3.

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2093:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2094:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2095:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2096:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2097:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2098:docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md:7:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2099:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2104:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:99:+- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2105:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:100:+  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2106:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:101:+  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2107:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:102:+  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:103:+  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2109:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:104:+  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2110:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:105:+  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2111:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:162:+| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2112:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:175:+2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2116:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:228:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2119:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:230:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2120:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:231:+| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2121:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:293:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2122:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:311:+**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2123:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:318:+The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2124:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:344:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:387:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2127:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:392:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2129:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2130:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2131:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2132:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:396:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2133:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2134:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2135:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2136:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2137:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2140:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:952:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2141:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2142:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2188:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3655:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2189:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3656:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2190:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3657:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2191:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3658:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2192:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3659:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2193:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3660:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2194:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3661:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2195:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3662:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2196:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3663:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2199:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3666:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2200:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3667:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2201:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3668:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.

C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2214:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3807:    54	## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2215:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3809:    56	C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2216:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3815:    62	The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2217:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3860:    34	The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2218:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3912:    34	**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2220:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3987:    39	- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2292:   240	## 8. Doctrine review checklist
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2294:   242	Full D0–D5 line-item-to-scenario mapping in
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2297:   245	| Doctrine | Proven by |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2299:   247	| **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2300:   248	| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2301:   249	| **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2302:   250	| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2303:   251	| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2304:   252	| **D5** capabilities report, never decide | CapabilityModule code proof + S6 (relay capability lifecycle storms) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2310:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2312:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2320:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2324:    45	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2333:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2339:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2352:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2354:    75	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2356:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2373:   240	## 8. Doctrine review checklist

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2380:   247	| **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2381:   248	| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2382:   249	| **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2383:   250	| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2384:   251	| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2385:   252	| **D5** capabilities report, never decide | CapabilityModule code proof + S6 (relay capability lifecycle storms) |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2558:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:280:| D2 | PASS | S2/S3/S8 metrics.json | <name> | <date> |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2559:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:281:| D3 | PASS | S7 metrics.json + toast-bridge merge SHA | <name> | <date> |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2560:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:283:| D5 | PASS | debt-inventory §3 D5 + S6 metrics.json + capability.rs code review | <name> | <date> |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:19:The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:33:Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:45:The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:75:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:87:**Secondary audience.** Existing Nostr client teams considering a port to Rust + multi-platform, who want a substrate they can compose rather than reimplement.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:180:| `nmp-core` | Kernel substrate: actor, store, planner, ledger, registries, extension traits, diagnostics | Pure Rust |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:200:The v1 release does **not** ship every module above as a finished product module. Per [`docs/plan.md`](../plan.md), v1 first proves the kernel substrate and codegen with a non-Nostr fixture module, then ships the Twitter-clone slice as protocol/app modules over the kernel.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:235:The proof app is the substrate for cross-platform consistency tests (§3.5): the same scripted action sequence runs against the proof app on all four platforms and `AppState` JSON snapshots must match.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:5:> **Companion docs:** [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md); `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/design/lmdb/gc.md:129:1. The view module's `dependencies(spec)` is consulted (per `kernel-substrate.md` §3).
docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:141:This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:85:+- **M0 (kernel substrate + non-Nostr fixture):** ✅ DONE.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:99:+- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:100:+  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:101:+  - **D1** Best-effort rendering: placeholders → in-place refinement, never withhold known data
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:102:+  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:103:+  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:104:+  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:105:+  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:111:+- **Post-merge codex review.** After every push to master, `codex exec` reviews the diff against the doctrine + file-size rules + spec coherence. Codex output saved to `docs/perf/codex-reviews/<sha>.md`. Any real concerns become fix-it TaskList entries.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:151:+- **Codex review on every merge.** `codex exec` is the post-merge doctrine reviewer. Saved transcripts in `docs/perf/codex-reviews/`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:162:+| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:167:+| `crates/` | `nmp-core` (substrate), `nmp-codegen` (per-app FFI crate generator), `nmp-testing` (mock relay, harnesses, scenarios), `fixture-todo-core` (non-Nostr extension-module proof). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:175:+2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:194:+> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:196:+> **Scope:** Enumerate every behavior the framework guarantees so the application does not have to author code for it. The user directive is explicit: *"apps shouldn't have to care or know about these operations happening in the background, things should just work."* This document is the contract; the test suite is the proof; the milestone implementations are the substrate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:202:+- [Intro — purpose, doctrine alignment, per-bullet template, how this contract evolves](framework-magic/intro.md)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:215:+Each row binds a behavior to: the sub-file that specifies it, the test name in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`, the milestone that owns the implementation, and the doctrine clause it discharges.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:217:+| # | Behavior | Sub-file | Test name | Milestone | Doctrine / spec |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:227:+| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:228:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:230:+| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:231:+| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:281:+- This document does **not** describe the proof app, the starter app, or the kernel-substrate trait families — those are the substrate the contract holds the framework to.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:293:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:295:+The "capabilities" filename in the user's directive maps here, not to capability bridges. The rendering contract is the **rendering capability** the framework grants the app: render now, refine in place, never withhold cached data behind a spinner. (Capability bridges in the technical sense — `KeyringCapability` etc. — are covered as plumbing in C11 and `kernel-substrate.md` §5; they are not themselves a contract bullet because they are infrastructure, not an observable app guarantee.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:306:+- The in-place refinement mechanism: `ViewModule::on_projection_changed` (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 148–150). When a kind:0 lands for author X, the kernel's projection cache (a shared cross-view projection) updates X's display name; every view module that lists items by X re-runs `on_projection_changed`, produces a delta, and the wire-emitter sends a `ViewBatch` with the updated field.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:307:+- The platform-shadow domain key (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 128 `fn key(spec: &Self::Spec) -> Self::Key`) ensures the cell-level re-render is targeted: the platform's reactive primitive updates only the row whose key matches, not the entire list.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:311:+**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:318:+The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:330:+3. **In-place refinement on time:** advance the `SimulatedClock` by 5 minutes; trigger the per-tick re-format (per `kernel-substrate.md` §3 `fn on_tick` line 153). Assert `items[0].created_at_display` updates from "just now" to "5 min ago" without the row being torn down.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:334:+**Milestone owner:** **[DONE]** for the placeholder shape (the M1 timeline slice already ships non-`Option` author fields with shortened-npub fallback — verified in `crates/nmp-core` timeline tests today). **[PENDING M2/M3]** for the full in-place refinement guarantees: sub-paths 1 and 5 are testable today; sub-paths 2 and 4 require the kernel's projection cache (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 148 `on_projection_changed`) which graduates in M2 alongside the view-module surface; sub-path 3 requires the per-tick re-format hook (`fn on_tick`, M2's `ViewModule` trait work).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:342:+## Doctrine alignment
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:344:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:352:+- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 — `ViewModule` trait including `on_projection_changed`, `on_tick`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:367:+# Framework Magic §1 — Intro, Doctrine Alignment, Per-Bullet Template
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:380:+1. A doctrine clause in `docs/aim.md` §6 or `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:385:+## 2. Doctrine alignment
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:387:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:389:+| Cardinal doctrine | Contract bullets it requires |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:392:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:396:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:398:+The older `aim.md` §6 doctrines map similarly: doctrines 4 (replaceable invariants) → C1/C2/C3; doctrine 5 (outbox auto, manual is opt-out) → C5/C6/C7; doctrine 6 (subs auto-group/auto-close/auto-dedup/auto-buffer) → C8; doctrine 7 (sessions are state, switching is an action) → C11/C12; doctrine 10 (provenance preserved) → C9.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:443:+- Not a doctrine source. The doctrines live in `docs/aim.md` §6 and `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5; this contract derives from them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:449:+The contract is dense in cross-references because the alternative — restating the cited material — would (a) violate the LOC ceiling, (b) drift, and (c) duplicate the existing design docs the milestones already own. Every chapter therefore reads as a thin layer over already-specified mechanism, with the contract's value-add being the **App writes / Test name / Doctrine** triple per bullet.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:455:+3. The chapter for the doctrine you care about.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:483:+5. The view payload's `items` recompute reactively per the standard `on_event_inserted` path (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3). No view handle is destroyed; the platform shadow's `useFollowingTimeline()` rune/observable continues to emit, just with a new payload.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:487:+**Failure mode prevented:** the canonical NDK-era bug: app code listens for kind:3 events, manually closes its open subscriptions, re-derives author lists, re-issues REQs, and either races itself (REQ ordering vs. local-state ordering) or leaks the old REQ. This contract structurally forbids that pattern: the view module never sees the kind:3 directly, and the app never issues a REQ. Specifically discharges aim.md §6 doctrine 6 ("subscriptions auto-group, auto-close, auto-dedup, auto-buffer; the developer never writes grouping/dedup/cleanup code") for the follow-list-change case.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:520:+`TBD-from-research(applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md)`: cite the query-builder API shape that lets a consumer phrase `"things kind:1 by people I follow"` once and get a stream that re-evaluates on every kind:3 change. Applesauce's mechanism is a builder that registers itself as a dependent of the kind:3 projection; the contract's `ViewModule.dependencies()` is the NMP analog (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 131–132). The research-fold commit cross-validates that the analog covers Applesauce's pattern fully.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:546:+Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:582:+**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:594:+## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:596:+C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:607:+- The action ledger row schema — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 owns it. C7 cares that the ledger correlates the per-relay attempts; the contract does not specify the row layout.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:621:+This chapter holds four bullets, all of which discharge `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 **bug-extinction #1** ("Stale replaceable event retained in state after a newer one arrives") and `docs/aim.md` §6 **doctrine 4** ("replaceable-event invariants enforced on insert"). The four are split because they cover four distinct kind-class shapes and have four distinct test surfaces.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:631:+**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #1. Plus the doctrine-4 footgun: an app caches kind:3 in its own state, fails to re-fetch on UI nav, renders a stale follow list, double-subscribes on the next session.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:661:+**App writes:** nothing. The view payloads recompute (via `ViewModule::on_event_removed` per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 141–143) and the deleted note disappears from `TimelineView.items` in the next emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:717:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4 (`SessionState`); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2 trigger A4 (`ActiveAccountChanged`); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.3 (account scope binding); `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 7.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:729:+- The signer attached to operations dispatched after the switch is the new active account's signer (per `IdentityModule` routing in `kernel-substrate.md` §6).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:744:+8. **Assert no overlap:** read the audit log of any per-account domain-store namespace (e.g., Alice's drafts) and assert Bob cannot read it. The kernel's domain-store isolation per account is the structural enforcement (`kernel-substrate.md` §8 "Domain stores are isolated" and the per-account scoping in domain key prefixes).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:750:+The eight sub-paths assert different facets of one observable contract: *after the switch dispatch, every consequence is a derived re-emission, never an imperative reissue.* The kernel-substrate (`kernel-substrate.md` §8) ensures domain-store isolation; the planner (`subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2) ensures interest re-resolution; the identity machinery (`kernel-substrate.md` §6) ensures signer rebinding. The contract bullet covers all three as one because they are observed together: an app that does `dispatch(SwitchActiveAccount)` and then attempts any operation gets a correctly-rebound system; partial rebinding is a regression.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:752:+## Doctrine alignment
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:756:+It also discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 7: "Sessions are state, switching is an action. No imperative 'log out, then log in, then reload' dance." That sentence is the contract C12 holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:762:+- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §8 — module composition rules, specifically domain-store isolation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:776:+- **Logging out / removing an account.** A `RemoveAccount` action exists in the long-term catalog (`subsystems.md` §7.4 implied); its contract surface is a separate potential bullet, not in v1's 13. Removal cleanly through the same `IdentityModule::destroy` path (kernel-substrate.md §6 line 341).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:786:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4 (sessions + signer catalog); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §5 (`CapabilityModule`), §6 (`IdentityModule`); `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` §"Folded into M6".
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:800:+- `IdentityModule` (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §6) is the trait family that hosts the local-key and bunker signers. The kernel owns identity ID assignment, secure-store persistence, and session activation routing (kernel-substrate.md §6 last paragraph).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:801:+- `KeyringCapability` (`kernel-substrate.md` §5 lines 305–308) is the kernel-provided capability that wraps macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Secret Service / Android Keystore. Capability calls report; they do not decide.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:802:+- The NIP-46 rendezvous flow is the `nostr-connect` crate's behavior; the framework wraps it as an `ActionModule` with the standard ledger-correlated capability-await pattern (`kernel-substrate.md` §4 `AwaitCapability` transition).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:807:+**Failure mode prevented:** the constellation of "DIY signer onboarding" bugs that every Nostr-on-mobile app re-discovers — leaked plaintext nsec in app state during the encryption window, lost bunker connection on app suspend, race between persistence and session activation, partial-failure leaving an `Account` in `SessionState` with no usable signer. The action ledger's atomicity (`kernel-substrate.md` §4 "Atomicity" paragraph) makes the "partial success" path explicit and recoverable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:834:+- External Android Amber via NIP-55 — wired via the `ExternalSignerCapability` (`kernel-substrate.md` §5); not a v1-ladder contract bullet because Android is M15.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:842:+This bullet is a load-bearing demonstration of the bible's capability pattern (aim.md §6 doctrine 11: "capabilities, not callbacks"). The KeyringCapability **reports** (here is the stored bytes; persistence succeeded/failed). It does **not decide** (whether to retry, whether to fall back to a different storage backend, whether to surface a UI prompt). The framework decides; the capability executes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:844:+The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is the structural witness for `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 (bounded native state) and for the implicit "secrets stay in Rust" rule — the platform layer never sees the unencrypted key material because every read/write of the key goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:848:+- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §6 — `IdentityModule` trait definition.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:849:+- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §5 — `CapabilityModule` framing + the named `KeyringCapability` family.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:952:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1196:+- **The reverse mapping from `AppAction` variants to action-ledger rows.** That's `kernel-substrate.md` §4 territory; the harness exposes `action_ledger()` and the test reads rows by index/id.

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1408:+This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3248:docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:55:`TBD-from-research(applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md)`: cite the query-builder API shape that lets a consumer phrase `"things kind:1 by people I follow"` once and get a stream that re-evaluates on every kind:3 change. Applesauce's mechanism is a builder that registers itself as a dependent of the kind:3 projection; the contract's `ViewModule.dependencies()` is the NMP analog (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 131–132). The research-fold commit cross-validates that the analog covers Applesauce's pattern fully.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3596:    92	- This document does **not** describe the proof app, the starter app, or the kernel-substrate trait families — those are the substrate the contract holds the framework to.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3612:    19	## 2. Doctrine alignment
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3614:    21	The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3616:    23	| Cardinal doctrine | Contract bullets it requires |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3618:    25	| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3619:    26	| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3620:    27	| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3621:    28	| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3622:    29	| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3623:    30	| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3625:    32	The older `aim.md` §6 doctrines map similarly: doctrines 4 (replaceable invariants) → C1/C2/C3; doctrine 5 (outbox auto, manual is opt-out) → C5/C6/C7; doctrine 6 (subs auto-group/auto-close/auto-dedup/auto-buffer) → C8; doctrine 7 (sessions are state, switching is an action) → C11/C12; doctrine 10 (provenance preserved) → C9.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3648:README.md:101:2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3649:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3650:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3651:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3652:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
