docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3659:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3660:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3661:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3662:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3663:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3664:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3665:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3666:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:29:The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3667:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:55:C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3668:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:57:C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3670:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3671:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3672:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3673:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3674:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3675:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3698:    19	The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3706:    27	## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3710:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3712:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3720:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3724:    45	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3733:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3739:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3759:     6	Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3795:    42	**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3807:    54	## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3809:    56	C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3815:    62	The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3820:    67	- The action ledger row schema — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 owns it. C7 cares that the ledger correlates the per-relay attempts; the contract does not specify the row layout.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3860:    34	The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3912:    34	**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3958:    10	| **M12 — Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)** | **Deferred to post-v1.** Wallet is huge surface area (NWC, NIP-57, NIP-60, NIP-61) and not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3959:    11	| *(none)* | **New M11.5 — Highlighter rebuild + `nmp-nip29` crate.** Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app proves the boundary. Source: `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs; the rebuild port it onto NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern). Adds NIP-29 as a first-class protocol module crate (groups, moderation, joining flow, kind 39000–39003 metadata events) done in NMP-idiomatic shape. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3979:    31	A dedicated **doctrine document** + **kernel-substrate test suite** that enumerates every behavior the framework guarantees without the app authoring code for it. Working title: "Things that just work." Includes (non-exhaustive):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3987:    39	- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4001:    53	3. **Framework-magic designer** — define the "things that just work" contract + the kernel-substrate test scaffolding for it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4015:    67	M0    Kernel substrate + fixture                                      ✅ DONE
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4045:README.md:28:  - **D2** Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4046:README.md:29:  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4047:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4050:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4051:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4052:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4059:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4065:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4078:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4080:    75	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4082:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4092:    87	**Secondary audience.** Existing Nostr client teams considering a port to Rust + multi-platform, who want a substrate they can compose rather than reimplement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4114:   111	In our Rust framing, the actor owns the event store as internal substrate. `AppState` is not the store; it is the bounded UI projection of currently open views plus small app metadata. The full event store never crosses FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4194:   179	Each payload type carries **pre-formatted** display strings (timestamps in user locale, npub-shortened forms, sat amounts). Per bible doctrine: no platform-side formatting.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4196:   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4326:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4524:     6	This chapter holds four bullets, all of which discharge `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 **bug-extinction #1** ("Stale replaceable event retained in state after a newer one arrives") and `docs/aim.md` §6 **doctrine 4** ("replaceable-event invariants enforced on insert"). The four are split because they cover four distinct kind-class shapes and have four distinct test surfaces.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4534:    16	**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #1. Plus the doctrine-4 footgun: an app caches kind:3 in its own state, fails to re-fetch on UI nav, renders a stale follow list, double-subscribes on the next session.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4564:    46	**App writes:** nothing. The view payloads recompute (via `ViewModule::on_event_removed` per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 141–143) and the deleted note disappears from `TimelineView.items` in the next emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4585:crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/identity.rs:61:    pub created_at: u64,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4618:crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/view.rs:11:    pub created_at: u64,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4629:     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4631:     6	The "capabilities" filename in the user's directive maps here, not to capability bridges. The rendering contract is the **rendering capability** the framework grants the app: render now, refine in place, never withhold cached data behind a spinner. (Capability bridges in the technical sense — `KeyringCapability` etc. — are covered as plumbing in C11 and `kernel-substrate.md` §5; they are not themselves a contract bullet because they are infrastructure, not an observable app guarantee.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4642:    17	- The in-place refinement mechanism: `ViewModule::on_projection_changed` (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 148–150). When a kind:0 lands for author X, the kernel's projection cache (a shared cross-view projection) updates X's display name; every view module that lists items by X re-runs `on_projection_changed`, produces a delta, and the wire-emitter sends a `ViewBatch` with the updated field.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4643:    18	- The platform-shadow domain key (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 128 `fn key(spec: &Self::Spec) -> Self::Key`) ensures the cell-level re-render is targeted: the platform's reactive primitive updates only the row whose key matches, not the entire list.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4647:    22	**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4654:    29	The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4666:    41	3. **In-place refinement on time:** advance the `SimulatedClock` by 5 minutes; trigger the per-tick re-format (per `kernel-substrate.md` §3 `fn on_tick` line 153). Assert `items[0].created_at_display` updates from "just now" to "5 min ago" without the row being torn down.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4670:    45	**Milestone owner:** **[DONE]** for the placeholder shape (the M1 timeline slice already ships non-`Option` author fields with shortened-npub fallback — verified in `crates/nmp-core` timeline tests today). **[PENDING M2/M3]** for the full in-place refinement guarantees: sub-paths 1 and 5 are testable today; sub-paths 2 and 4 require the kernel's projection cache (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 148 `on_projection_changed`) which graduates in M2 alongside the view-module surface; sub-path 3 requires the per-tick re-format hook (`fn on_tick`, M2's `ViewModule` trait work).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5188:docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:55:`TBD-from-research(applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md)`: cite the query-builder API shape that lets a consumer phrase `"things kind:1 by people I follow"` once and get a stream that re-evaluates on every kind:3 change. Applesauce's mechanism is a builder that registers itself as a dependent of the kind:3 projection; the contract's `ViewModule.dependencies()` is the NMP analog (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 131–132). The research-fold commit cross-validates that the analog covers Applesauce's pattern fully.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5560:   141	This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5613:- [README.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/README.md:25) vs [docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54): D0-D5 are inconsistent. README says D2 reactivity, D3 errors-never-FFI, D5 capabilities-report; product spec and framework-magic use D2 negentropy, D3 outbox, D5 snapshots. Fix one canonical doctrine map, then update framework-magic citations.

docs/plan/m4-negentropy.md:3:> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Arc 1 — Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:15:You are reviewing merge 55dd5f2 on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:17:- D1: best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place; no spinners gating renderable content
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:18:- D2: negentropy first, REQ second; every filter/relay pair is a tracked sync target
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:19:- D3: outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:20:- D4: single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:21:- D5: snapshots bounded by what's open; AppState carries view payloads only for open views
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:42:-> **Three arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:43:+> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:48:+> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:84:+  - Error-shape exhaustion: every typed FFI error path exercised; assert each one becomes a `toast: Option<String>` state field, never a thrown exception across the boundary (D3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:108:+- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:238: | **Non-Nostr data bridge** | M0 (substrate), M10 (long-running capabilities), M11 (podcast app proves it in production) | DomainModule trait + ADR-0007 bridge lanes; first proven by fixture-todo-core; production proof in podcast app. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:263:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) file-size budget, (4) internal consistency with the plan's own stated scope. Be terse. If everything is fine, say so. If there is a real concern, name the file:line and what to fix.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:265:I’ll verify the merged file directly so the review can cite current line numbers rather than relying on the pasted diff. I’m checking the plan text, file length, and TODO-style markers against the stated doctrine/scope.

Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:783:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:787:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:789:33-Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:795:41:### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:799:45:The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:805:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:811:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:820:73:### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:822:75:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:824:77:### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:865:    19	The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:873:    27	## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:877:    31	### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:879:    33	Per ADR-0009, NMP is a Nostr-native app kernel plus extension modules. The kernel provides substrate; protocol modules and app modules contribute typed variants via `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `DomainModule`, `CapabilityModule`, and `IdentityModule`. If implementing a real app requires adding domain nouns to `nmp-core`, the kernel boundary is wrong and must change.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:887:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:891:    45	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:900:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:906:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:919:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:921:    75	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:923:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:933:    87	**Secondary audience.** Existing Nostr client teams considering a port to Rust + multi-platform, who want a substrate they can compose rather than reimplement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1026:   180	| `nmp-core` | Kernel substrate: actor, store, planner, ledger, registries, extension traits, diagnostics | Pure Rust |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1031:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1032:19:- **Kernel substrate** in `crates/nmp-core` (~3,800 LOC): actor on a dedicated OS thread, mailbox-driven (ADR feedback adopted — relay reads happen in tokio reader tasks, the actor blocks on its own channel with deadline timeouts), substrate trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`) in `nmp-core/src/substrate/`, ingest pipeline (`kernel/ingest.rs`), claim/release refcounting for profile interest (commit `23ae829`), composite reverse-index dependency tracking.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1137:   650	| **Non-Nostr data bridge** | M0 (substrate), M10 (long-running capabilities), M11 (podcast app proves it in production) | DomainModule trait + ADR-0007 bridge lanes; first proven by fixture-todo-core; production proof in podcast app. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1183:20-- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1184:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/55dd5f2.md:1188:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/design/lmdb/tests.md:164:### 2.13 Domain isolation (`kernel-substrate.md` §8)
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:1:# Design: Kernel substrate (extension trait families)
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:173:- Pre-formatted display fields per doctrine D1.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:237:**Atomicity** (doctrine guarantee): the kernel ensures `module.reduce(...)`, ledger transitions, and local store writes happen as one actor message. External effects such as relay publishes cannot be rolled back after a relay accepts them, so publish steps must be ledger-correlated and restart-recoverable: a "publish accepted but local insert failed" path becomes an explicit failed/recovery ledger state, not silent divergence.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:437:**v1 kernel substrate** (Phase 1a.1):
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:483:1. Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) ships with one fixture module (`fixture-todo-core`) demonstrating each of the five trait families. Codegen produces a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate. Desktop iced app renders a TODO list, no business logic in Swift / iced.
docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:3:> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Arc 1 — Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack.
docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md:14:- ✅ Best-effort rendering (D1): placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival.
docs/aim.md:5:This document is the cold-start context for a brand-new working session. Read it before doing anything else. It defines the project's north star, architectural foundation, the bodies of prior work being synthesized, the doctrine the design must enforce, and the crate layout. It does not contain implementation. It contains the *aim*.
docs/aim.md:111:In our Rust framing, the actor owns the event store as internal substrate. `AppState` is not the store; it is the bounded UI projection of currently open views plus small app metadata. The full event store never crosses FFI.
docs/aim.md:219:## 6. Doctrine — the rules the API must make hard to violate
docs/plan/m7-interaction-loop.md:3:> Part of the [Build & Validation Plan](../plan.md). Arc 1 — Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:1:# Framework Magic §1 — Intro, Doctrine Alignment, Per-Bullet Template
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:14:1. A doctrine clause in `docs/aim.md` §6 or `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:19:## 2. Doctrine alignment
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:23:| Cardinal doctrine | Contract bullets it requires |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:26:| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:28:| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:29:| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:30:| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:32:The older `aim.md` §6 doctrines map similarly: doctrines 4 (replaceable invariants) → C1/C2/C3; doctrine 5 (outbox auto, manual is opt-out) → C5/C6/C7; doctrine 6 (subs auto-group/auto-close/auto-dedup/auto-buffer) → C8; doctrine 7 (sessions are state, switching is an action) → C11/C12; doctrine 10 (provenance preserved) → C9.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:77:- Not a doctrine source. The doctrines live in `docs/aim.md` §6 and `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5; this contract derives from them.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:83:The contract is dense in cross-references because the alternative — restating the cited material — would (a) violate the LOC ceiling, (b) drift, and (c) duplicate the existing design docs the milestones already own. Every chapter therefore reads as a thin layer over already-specified mechanism, with the contract's value-add being the **App writes / Test name / Doctrine** triple per bullet.
docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:89:3. The chapter for the doctrine you care about.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4 (sessions + signer catalog); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §5 (`CapabilityModule`), §6 (`IdentityModule`); `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` §"Folded into M6".
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:18:- `IdentityModule` (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §6) is the trait family that hosts the local-key and bunker signers. The kernel owns identity ID assignment, secure-store persistence, and session activation routing (kernel-substrate.md §6 last paragraph).
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:19:- `KeyringCapability` (`kernel-substrate.md` §5 lines 305–308) is the kernel-provided capability that wraps macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Secret Service / Android Keystore. Capability calls report; they do not decide.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:20:- The NIP-46 rendezvous flow is the `nostr-connect` crate's behavior; the framework wraps it as an `ActionModule` with the standard ledger-correlated capability-await pattern (`kernel-substrate.md` §4 `AwaitCapability` transition).
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:25:**Failure mode prevented:** the constellation of "DIY signer onboarding" bugs that every Nostr-on-mobile app re-discovers — leaked plaintext nsec in app state during the encryption window, lost bunker connection on app suspend, race between persistence and session activation, partial-failure leaving an `Account` in `SessionState` with no usable signer. The action ledger's atomicity (`kernel-substrate.md` §4 "Atomicity" paragraph) makes the "partial success" path explicit and recoverable.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:52:- External Android Amber via NIP-55 — wired via the `ExternalSignerCapability` (`kernel-substrate.md` §5); not a v1-ladder contract bullet because Android is M15.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:60:This bullet is a load-bearing demonstration of the bible's capability pattern (aim.md §6 doctrine 11: "capabilities, not callbacks"). The KeyringCapability **reports** (here is the stored bytes; persistence succeeded/failed). It does **not decide** (whether to retry, whether to fall back to a different storage backend, whether to surface a UI prompt). The framework decides; the capability executes.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:62:The test's assertion that no plaintext nsec crosses FFI is the structural witness for `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 (bounded native state) and for the implicit "secrets stay in Rust" rule — the platform layer never sees the unencrypted key material because every read/write of the key goes through the in-Rust `IdentityModule::sign` function.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:66:- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §6 — `IdentityModule` trait definition.
docs/design/framework-magic/signers.md:67:- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §5 — `CapabilityModule` framing + the named `KeyringCapability` family.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:6:> **Prerequisites:** [`docs/plan/m11-podcast.md`](../plan/m11-podcast.md), `docs/aim.md`, `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md`, `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md`, `docs/decisions/0009`, `docs/decisions/0010`, `crates/fixture-todo-core/src/lib.rs`.
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:13:A **pixel-perfect rebuild** of the canonical Swift podcast app at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (8,793 LOC across 47 Swift files, see [`inventory.md`](podcast/inventory.md)) running on NMP. Every Swift view file in `PodcastApp/Views/` is copied verbatim into `ios/NmpPodcast/Views/`; only the data source is rewired. All business logic — services, models, view models, the LLM/RAG/transcription pipeline, audio orchestration, downloads, RSS, recommendations — moves into Rust extension modules behind the kernel substrate (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`).
docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:108:Plus seven new capability families landed in `nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/` (each pure-trait; impls live in the platform shell). See [`podcast/capabilities.md`](podcast/capabilities.md).
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4 (`SessionState`); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2 trigger A4 (`ActiveAccountChanged`); `docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.3 (account scope binding); `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 7.
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:16:- The signer attached to operations dispatched after the switch is the new active account's signer (per `IdentityModule` routing in `kernel-substrate.md` §6).
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:31:8. **Assert no overlap:** read the audit log of any per-account domain-store namespace (e.g., Alice's drafts) and assert Bob cannot read it. The kernel's domain-store isolation per account is the structural enforcement (`kernel-substrate.md` §8 "Domain stores are isolated" and the per-account scoping in domain key prefixes).
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:37:The eight sub-paths assert different facets of one observable contract: *after the switch dispatch, every consequence is a derived re-emission, never an imperative reissue.* The kernel-substrate (`kernel-substrate.md` §8) ensures domain-store isolation; the planner (`subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.2) ensures interest re-resolution; the identity machinery (`kernel-substrate.md` §6) ensures signer rebinding. The contract bullet covers all three as one because they are observed together: an app that does `dispatch(SwitchActiveAccount)` and then attempts any operation gets a correctly-rebound system; partial rebinding is a regression.
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:39:## Doctrine alignment
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:43:It also discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 7: "Sessions are state, switching is an action. No imperative 'log out, then log in, then reload' dance." That sentence is the contract C12 holds in place.
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:49:- `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §8 — module composition rules, specifically domain-store isolation.
docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:63:- **Logging out / removing an account.** A `RemoveAccount` action exists in the long-term catalog (`subsystems.md` §7.4 implied); its contract surface is a separate potential bullet, not in v1's 13. Removal cleanly through the same `IdentityModule::destroy` path (kernel-substrate.md §6 line 341).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/b4b6afa.md:3:**Merge:** docs(m11): fix codex feedback (file sizes, harness, D5, D4, rig.rs gate, OPML, counts)
docs/plan/principles.md:8:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/plan/principles.md:9:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the [M11](m11-podcast.md) podcast-app proof.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:42:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #3 ("Publish of an event to relays the author has not declared as write relays") and bug #4 ("DM published to public relays"). Plus the doctrine-10 footgun: a "send everywhere" fallback that publishes a gift wrap to the global content relay because the recipient's inbox lookup returned empty.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:67:- The action ledger row schema — `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 owns it. C7 cares that the ledger correlates the per-relay attempts; the contract does not specify the row layout.
docs/plan/post-v1.md:9:See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:5:> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:7:> **Scope:** Enumerate every behavior the framework guarantees so the application does not have to author code for it. The user directive is explicit: *"apps shouldn't have to care or know about these operations happening in the background, things should just work."* This document is the contract; the test suite is the proof; the milestone implementations are the substrate.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:13:- [Intro — purpose, doctrine alignment, per-bullet template, how this contract evolves](framework-magic/intro.md)
docs/design/framework-magic.md:26:Each row binds a behavior to: the sub-file that specifies it, the test name in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`, the milestone that owns the implementation, and the doctrine clause it discharges.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:28:| # | Behavior | Sub-file | Test name | Milestone | Doctrine / spec |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:34:| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:35:| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:38:| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:92:- This document does **not** describe the proof app, the starter app, or the kernel-substrate trait families — those are the substrate the contract holds the framework to.
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:18:   (`docs/aim.md` §2 commandments 1–10), every cardinal doctrine
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:19:   (`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 D0–D5), and every ownership
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:66:| 8 | `nmp_app_open_author(*mut, *const c_char)` | `void nmp_app_open_author(void *app, const char *pubkey)` | UTF-8 C string, expected 64-char lowercase hex pubkey. Hex-validated via `is_hex_pubkey`. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace. Empty / non-hex inputs are **silently dropped** (see §7 finding D3-gap). | Silent no-op on null app or null pubkey. |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:112:| # | Scenario | Threading concern | Primary doctrine tested |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:114:| S1 | Mount/unmount churn | actor recv + refcount | D5 (snapshot bounded), bible #5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:116:| S3 | Snapshot pressure | listener serialization | bible #9 (≤60 Hz), D5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:117:| S4 | Reconciler back-pressure | listener channel growth | bible #9, D1 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:120:| S7 | Error-shape exhaustion | every invalid input path | D3 (no errors cross FFI) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:121:| S8 | Subscription planner DOS | OpenView/CloseView storm | D2 (≤60 Hz/view), D5 |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:122:| S9 | Relay flap | reconnect + watermark | bible #7, D2 |

docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:240:## 8. Doctrine review checklist
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:242:Full D0–D5 line-item-to-scenario mapping in
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:245:| Doctrine | Proven by |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:247:| **D0** kernel never grows app nouns | debt-inventory §3 D0 audit + S6 (the kernel does not grow capability variants under churn) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:248:| **D1** best-effort rendering with placeholders | S3 (snapshot pressure) + S4 (reconciler back-pressure) + code proof (ProfileCard.placeholder) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:249:| **D2** ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound | S2, S3, S8 — emit-rate cap, planner dedup |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:250:| **D3** errors never cross FFI | S7 (exhaustion) + §7.2 (toast bridge) |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:251:| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:252:| **D5** capabilities report, never decide | CapabilityModule code proof + S6 (relay capability lifecycle storms) |
docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:106:- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:6:This chapter holds four bullets, all of which discharge `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 **bug-extinction #1** ("Stale replaceable event retained in state after a newer one arrives") and `docs/aim.md` §6 **doctrine 4** ("replaceable-event invariants enforced on insert"). The four are split because they cover four distinct kind-class shapes and have four distinct test surfaces.
docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:16:**Failure mode prevented:** §3.3 bug #1. Plus the doctrine-4 footgun: an app caches kind:3 in its own state, fails to re-fetch on UI nav, renders a stale follow list, double-subscribes on the next session.
docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:46:**App writes:** nothing. The view payloads recompute (via `ViewModule::on_event_removed` per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 141–143) and the deleted note disappears from `TimelineView.items` in the next emit.
docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:18:5. The view payload's `items` recompute reactively per the standard `on_event_inserted` path (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3). No view handle is destroyed; the platform shadow's `useFollowingTimeline()` rune/observable continues to emit, just with a new payload.
docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:22:**Failure mode prevented:** the canonical NDK-era bug: app code listens for kind:3 events, manually closes its open subscriptions, re-derives author lists, re-issues REQs, and either races itself (REQ ordering vs. local-state ordering) or leaks the old REQ. This contract structurally forbids that pattern: the view module never sees the kind:3 directly, and the app never issues a REQ. Specifically discharges aim.md §6 doctrine 6 ("subscriptions auto-group, auto-close, auto-dedup, auto-buffer; the developer never writes grouping/dedup/cleanup code") for the follow-list-change case.
docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md:55:`TBD-from-research(applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md)`: cite the query-builder API shape that lets a consumer phrase `"things kind:1 by people I follow"` once and get a stream that re-evaluates on every kind:3 change. Applesauce's mechanism is a builder that registers itself as a dependent of the kind:3 projection; the contract's `ViewModule.dependencies()` is the NMP analog (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 lines 131–132). The research-fold commit cross-validates that the analog covers Applesauce's pattern fully.
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:10:| **M12 — Wallet (NWC + zaps + Cashu + nutzaps)** | **Deferred to post-v1.** Wallet is huge surface area (NWC, NIP-57, NIP-60, NIP-61) and not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:11:| *(none)* | **New M11.5 — Highlighter rebuild + `nmp-nip29` crate.** Builds the second non-social-domain extension app on top of the kernel, after the podcast app proves the boundary. Source: `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` (Highlighter — already has a Rust core + native UIs; the rebuild port it onto NMP's substrate + codegen + view-module/action-module pattern). Adds NIP-29 as a first-class protocol module crate (groups, moderation, joining flow, kind 39000–39003 metadata events) done in NMP-idiomatic shape. |
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:31:A dedicated **doctrine document** + **kernel-substrate test suite** that enumerates every behavior the framework guarantees without the app authoring code for it. Working title: "Things that just work." Includes (non-exhaustive):
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:39:- Profile placeholders → in-place refinement (D1; already done)
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:53:3. **Framework-magic designer** — define the "things that just work" contract + the kernel-substrate test scaffolding for it.
docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:67:M0    Kernel substrate + fixture                                      ✅ DONE
docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:15:You are reviewing merge 0dfb97581315aa04bd66341a98a32215de5e14d6 (the M11 podcast-app rebuild design) on master in nostr-multi-platform. Doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns, D1 best-effort rendering, D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view, D3 no errors across FFI, D4 one writer per fact, D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:89:+A **pixel-perfect rebuild** of the canonical Swift podcast app at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (8,793 LOC across 47 Swift files, see [`inventory.md`](podcast/inventory.md)) running on NMP. Every Swift view file in `PodcastApp/Views/` is copied verbatim into `ios/NmpPodcast/Views/`; only the data source is rewired. All business logic — services, models, view models, the LLM/RAG/transcription pipeline, audio orchestration, downloads, RSS, recommendations — moves into Rust extension modules behind the kernel substrate (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:184:+Plus seven new capability families landed in `nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/` (each pure-trait; impls live in the platform shell). See [`podcast/capabilities.md`](podcast/capabilities.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:244:+> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §5; doctrine: D5 (capabilities report, never decide).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:325:+### Bounded-state proof (D5)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:553:+All trait files live in `crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/`:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:556:+crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:895:+Every test file ≤ 500 LOC. The cross-cutting kill-relaunch test is the most load-bearing — it asserts D2 (reactivity) and D4 (single writer per fact) hold under app termination at every state-transition boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:905:+3. **`capability-review`** — runs `cargo run -p nmp-codegen -- validate-capabilities --capabilities-dir crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/capabilities/` — a new codegen subcommand that asserts request/result type definitions don't mention any podcast-domain noun (uses a curated wordlist + AST traversal).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:939:+- **Substrate kind**: which trait family in `nmp-core/src/substrate/` backs it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:993:+| `Utilities/ErrorPresentation.swift` | 58 | swift | `ios/NmpPodcast/Bridge/ErrorPresentation.swift` | UI-only — `AppError`/`ErrorHandler` map `toast: Option<String>` from `AppState` (doctrine D3). No business logic. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1182:+- "No business logic in native." (Doctrine D0 + AGENTS.md guardrails.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1206:+> Substrate reference: [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1441:+17 `ViewModule`s. Each in `podcast-core/src/views/<name>.rs`. All share `View*` payload conventions: pre-formatted strings (per doctrine D1), no business logic in Swift, composite-keyed `ViewDependencies` (per ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1577:+### D.8 Atomicity contract (per [`../kernel-substrate.md`](../kernel-substrate.md) §4)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1591:+Per ADR-0001, projection cache changes feed `ProjectionChange` events into `ViewModule::on_projection_changed`. Each is registered as a `ProjectionCache` in `nmp-core::substrate::projections` (kernel-owned trait; modules opt in).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1767:+All actions return parsed records. None of them write to the domain store directly — `podcast-core::SubscribePodcast`/`RefreshFeed` orchestrate the writes, preserving the doctrine: **one writer per fact** (D4).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2087:+- Failure-mode test: capability emits `Error { reason: "rate_limit" }` mid-stream → `ChatTurn` commits with `is_error: true` and the `toast` field is set per doctrine D3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2830:+- Acceptance: subscribe to a real RSS feed (Tim Ferriss) → library shows it → tap → see ≥ 5 episodes parsed; UI updates as `FetchFeed` action completes (no spinner gate; placeholder rows during fetch per doctrine D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:2973:1. D0 enforcement — does anything in the design leak podcast nouns into nmp-core? Are the new capabilities (Audio, Background, Embedding, KV) general-shape or accidentally podcast-shaped?

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3035:docs/plan.md:5:> **Four arcs:** Kernel substrate + Nostr social stack (M0–M10) → FFI hardening + iOS empirical proof (M10.5) → kernel-boundary proof with a non-social-domain app (M11, the **`../podcast` rebuild on NMP**) → wallet/WoT + cross-platform + release (M12–M17).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3037:docs/plan.md:20:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3041:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3107:docs/plan.md:650:| **Non-Nostr data bridge** | M0 (substrate), M10 (long-running capabilities), M11 (podcast app proves it in production) | DomainModule trait + ADR-0007 bridge lanes; first proven by fixture-todo-core; production proof in podcast app. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3129:docs/aim.md:111:In our Rust framing, the actor owns the event store as internal substrate. `AppState` is not the store; it is the bounded UI projection of currently open views plus small app metadata. The full event store never crosses FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3160:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:352:The kernel owns identity ID assignment, secure-store persistence, session activation, and routing of `dispatch(AppAction::Sign(...))` to the right module. Apps decide which identity scopes exist in their model.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3161:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:459:- `twitter-core`: the demo app (compose UI state, settings).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3162:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:485:3. A future Highlighter-lite, TENEX-lite, or podcast-lite module can be added without changes to `nmp-core` traits. Demonstrated on paper for v1; demonstrated in code post-v1.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3211:docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:13:A **pixel-perfect rebuild** of the canonical Swift podcast app at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` (8,793 LOC across 47 Swift files, see [`inventory.md`](podcast/inventory.md)) running on NMP. Every Swift view file in `PodcastApp/Views/` is copied verbatim into `ios/NmpPodcast/Views/`; only the data source is rewired. All business logic — services, models, view models, the LLM/RAG/transcription pipeline, audio orchestration, downloads, RSS, recommendations — moves into Rust extension modules behind the kernel substrate (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3493:A few issues are now concrete rather than stylistic: the design has at least one hard LOC conflict with the copy-verbatim requirement, and the screenshot gate currently depends on an agent-only UI introspection path. I’m pulling the exact lines for those plus the doctrine/D4 ownership concerns.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3978:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3980:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3997:3. [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:79): `AudioPlaybackBridge` decides repeated `Load` is a no-op based on `currentEpisode`. That is playback policy in native, and it conflicts with the same doc’s D5 claim at [capabilities.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:87) plus the lesson at [lessons.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:58). Fix: Rust decides whether to send `Load`; the bridge only executes requests and reports state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3999:4. [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:130), [podcast-core.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-core.md:231), and [podcast-rag.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/podcast/podcast-rag.md:132): `podcast-core` stores `embedding_id`, says it is re-exported from `podcast-rag`, while `podcast-rag` depends on `podcast-core` and backfills the core record. That creates either a crate cycle or a D4 one-writer violation. Fix: define `EmbeddingId` in `podcast-core` or a shared types crate, and have core own any writes to core records after `podcast-rag` returns `Indexed`.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:34:- domain store substrate for non-Nostr records,
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:77:NMP should expose a namespaced domain-store substrate:
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:262:If multiple apps repeat a pattern, extract the deterministic substrate, not the product noun. For example:
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:206:- **The reverse mapping from `AppAction` variants to action-ledger rows.** That's `kernel-substrate.md` §4 territory; the harness exposes `action_ledger()` and the test reads rows by index/id.
docs/plan/status.md:9:- **Kernel substrate** in `crates/nmp-core` (~3,800 LOC): actor on a dedicated OS thread, mailbox-driven (ADR feedback adopted — relay reads happen in tokio reader tasks, the actor blocks on its own channel with deadline timeouts), substrate trait families (`DomainModule`, `ViewModule`, `ActionModule`, `CapabilityModule`, `IdentityModule`) in `nmp-core/src/substrate/`, ingest pipeline (`kernel/ingest.rs`), claim/release refcounting for profile interest (commit `23ae829`), composite reverse-index dependency tracking.
docs/plan/status.md:10:- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/plan/status.md:11:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:779:d660735:docs/aim.md:111:In our Rust framing, the actor owns the event store as internal substrate. `AppState` is not the store; it is the bounded UI projection of currently open views plus small app metadata. The full event store never crosses FFI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:802:d660735:docs/decisions/0006-vertical-slice-first.md:112:- **Subsequent expansion has a working substrate to build on.** Adding LMDB is a `Box<dyn EventStore>` swap. Adding multi-relay is a planner change. Adding negentropy is a planner change. Adding iOS is a UniFFI wrap of the existing actor. None require redesigning the architecture.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:827:d660735:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:81:The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:861:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:196:    /// Drive the state machine. Input could be a capability result, a relay OK,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:862:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:289:Wraps the bible's capability bridge pattern. The kernel ships a small set of generic capability families; modules extend with app-specific ones.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:863:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:305:**Kernel-provided capability families** (each is a `CapabilityModule`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:864:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:316:**Module-provided capability examples:**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:865:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:371:  src/capability.rs ✓ (8 capability traits)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:866:d660735:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:391:- **Capabilities are module-private.** A capability registered by Module A is callable only by code that has a typed handle to it. The kernel does not route across.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:948:d660735:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:986:d660735:docs/plan.md:650:| **Non-Nostr data bridge** | M0 (substrate), M10 (long-running capabilities), M11 (podcast app proves it in production) | DomainModule trait + ADR-0007 bridge lanes; first proven by fixture-todo-core; production proof in podcast app. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1028:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:19:The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1031:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:45:The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1032:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54:### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1034:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:60:### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1035:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:75:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1043:d660735:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:235:The proof app is the substrate for cross-platform consistency tests (§3.5): the same scripted action sequence runs against the proof app on all four platforms and `AppState` JSON snapshots must match.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1055:d660735:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:181:**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1058:d660735:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:242:Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1099:    41	### D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1103:    45	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1112:    54	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1118:    60	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1131:    73	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1133:    75	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1135:    77	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1159:    14	- Build them in Swift/Kotlin. Violates the doctrine ("no business logic in native code") the framework exists to enforce.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1174:    29	- Domain-store substrate for non-Nostr records.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1193:    48	| `nmp-core` kernel | actor, store substrate, planner, ledger, registries, codegen, diagnostics | No |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1198:    53	**Five extension trait families** (concrete signatures in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1217:    72	The reactive machinery and platform shadow doctrine are independent of who owns the noun:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1226:    81	The doctrines from `product-spec.md` §1.5 (D1 best-effort rendering, D2 negentropy first, D3 outbox automatic, D4 single writer per fact, D5 snapshots bounded by what's open) survive intact and apply across all modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1232:    87	- **Phase 1a takes longer.** ADR-0008's 8-week estimate grows to roughly 12–15 weeks. The kernel substrate (1a.1) and tiny fixture module land before the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Twitter clone follows on top.
